JARVIS-LTM · OPTION MAP & RESEARCH LIBRARY

updated 2026-07-11
HOW TO USE THIS DOCUMENT (for humans and for LLMs reading it cold): This is the complete, self-contained option map of the Jarvis-LTM research program — every path chosen, staged, held open, sent for an operator decision, or killed, with receipts. Controlling document: ltm/PLAN.md (Plan of Record v2, approved 2026-07-10) — nothing on this page overrides it or LOCAL_POLICY_CONFLICTS.md. Rules of inheritance: (1) killed lanes are killed — a lane may be reconsidered ONLY if its graveyard entry is tagged KILLED-REEXAMINABLE and the operator explicitly signs off on a genuinely new mechanism class with a pre-committed falsifier; (2) items tagged DECISION-NEEDED are not candidates — they await an operator scope ruling and must not be built speculatively; (3) nothing here is evidence of profitability — everything staged ends at SHADOW-READY, and forward performance is the only real judge. Namespace warning: AIS experiment cards are cited CARD-E01…E18; recovery labels REC-E01…E48 are a different namespace — never join on the bare label (LPC #12). Option IDs OPT-001…217 refer to ltm/research/RESEARCH_OPTION_INVENTORY_2026_07_11.md; plan lines PLN-001…228 to PLAN_COVERAGE_INVENTORY_2026_07_11.md.

Click a phase (top row) or a 2026-07-11 surface (second row) to open its option tree.

CHOSEN ACTIVE CANDIDATE ADOPTED-GAP STAGED PARKED DECISION-NEEDED BLOCKED QUARANTINED KILLED-CLEAN KILLED-REEXAMINABLE SUPERSEDED
→ → → → →

Phase 1 — DATA / EVENTS

Two decisions live here: which MOMENTS the model learns on (the training regime), and which RAW MATERIAL feeds everything.

Diagram A — what feeds the machine (sources → corpus builder → the corpus law)
HAVEOHLCV bars
HAVEExternal context
STAGED · CONDTick data + buyer/seller flag
CANDIDATE · LOWChart images
BLOCKED · no sourceText / news content
CANDIDATEDECISION/SCOPING NEEDEDSocial signal deck
KILLED-CLEANMBO / orderbook depth
KILLED-CLEAN 16/16Kronos embeddings
CORPUS BUILDER
honest engine (reconciliation gate)→real costs in→temporal splits→clean universe selection (no peeks)→two corpora: execution vs contrast (≤20% weight)
solid line = feeding the builder today · dashed grey = blocked, no source wired · red ✕ = killed source, connector severed (banned / failed)
Diagram B — whose moments do we learn on (4 real alternatives, one fork)
WHICH MOMENTS
DO WE LEARN ON?
CHOSEN · era 1Strategy-fire events
NEXT ERA ⭐All-bar action corpus
SUPERSEDEDHuman-labeled setups
PARKEDReinforcement learning
Diagram C — the custody timeline (v5 splits, 2021 → now, widths ∝ months)
TRAINCHOSEN · both instruments
OOSCHOSEN · two quarters
ROBUSTNESSDEMOTED · one-way read
TRUE VAULTSEALED · grows daily →
2021-01 → 2025-09both instruments · 4 regimes incl. the 2022 bear
2025-10 → 2026-03two quarters — kills the one-regime caveat
2026-04 → 2026-06demoted (design peeked) · opened once, read ONE-WAY: fail = alarm, pass = weak evidence only
2026-06-05 → nowmathematically untouched · min ~84 trading days before the ONE final read (~Oct earliest)
full detail (expand) — the original cards, verbatim
Training regime — which moments?
CHOSEN · era 1Strategy-fire eventslearn on entry-trigger moments: 151k strategy fires across 3 baskets (mean-reversion / breakout / hybrid), 5 years of ES and NQ, real costs included; 5m and 15m charts only — the 1-minute chart was cut because five years of data showed no edge there and the worst cost-vs-stop math
NEXT ERA ⭐All-bar action corpusevery 5m bar gets counterfactual long/short/flat outcomes under frozen geometries — the model MINTS strategies instead of filtering them
SUPERSEDEDHuman-labeled setupsbanned as ongoing input (custody + policy); 680 historical labels = archive only
PARKEDReinforcement learningsample-hungry, hard to audit
Raw inputs — what feeds the rows?
HAVEOHLCV bars10 yrs ES · 9 yrs NQ (2016+ found on disk) — the substrate
HAVEExternal contextVIX, dealer gamma, COT, Fed/CPI calendar — always lagged, never same-day
STAGED · CONDTick data + buyer/seller flagwe own ES tick data; the buy-vs-sell pressure block built from it goes ahead only after two checks — the vendor's buyer/seller flags verified on a backfill, and a statistical test that the feed behaves the same across eras. Uses executed trades only, not the banned order-book data [C17 · trap E5]
BLOCKED · no sourceText / news contentuntouched modality, no source wired; if ever opened: PIT language models only (OPT-026) + ALFRED vintages for macro (OPT-068)
CANDIDATEDECISION/SCOPING NEEDEDSocial signal deck (X/Twitter) + operator-taught copy-readingBuild a fast TweetDeck-style monitor — multi-column, real-time, faster than the real thing — over the accounts worth reading. The operator then teaches the signal patterns those accounts emit (what a real call looks like vs noise), we encode that reading, and the system copies the trades mechanically. Proposed by the operator 2026-07-11. Before any build this needs scoping: the text/social modality is currently unwired (no data source), any use must be point-in-time honest (a tweet counts only from its timestamp, no deleted-tweet survivorship), and signal-copying sits outside the current five bets until a falsifier and cost model are written. Operator note (07-11): a shortlist of accounts that do not delete is already identified — the survivorship trap is pre-mitigated; deck build, point-in-time rules and cost model remain.
CANDIDATE · LOWChart imagesprecedent exists; bars carry the same info
KILLED-CLEANMBO / orderbook depthsub-second lane failed repeatedly — banned (LPC #1: do not acquire, compute, store, or expand)
KILLED-CLEAN 16/16Kronos embeddingspretrained chart-read real (0.61 alone) but 93% redundant — hurt every fold
Splits (v5, custody-repaired)
TRAIN2021-01 → 2025-09both instruments, 4 regimes incl. the 2022 bear
OOS2025-10 → 2026-03two quarters — kills the one-regime caveat
ROBUSTNESS2026-04 → 2026-06demoted — earlier design choices had already peeked at this period; it gets opened once and read one-way: a fail is an alarm, a pass counts only as weak evidence
TRUE VAULT2026-06-05 → forwardmathematically untouched, grows daily; minimum ~84 trading days before the one final read (~Oct earliest)
How we build a corpus (the law of this phase) honest engine (reconciliation gate)→ real costs in→ temporal splits→ clean universe selection (no peeks)→ two corpora: execution vs contrast (≤20% weight)
Key finding: the corpus loses by cost geometry, not bad entries — gross ≈ break-even, costs ≈ the whole loss. New law: cost ≤ 10% of stop (ES stops ≥ 12.5pt, NQ ≥ 20pt) — designed in, not filtered after.

Phase 2 — FEATURES

Redone 2026-07-11 (feature-tier doctrine v1.2): the 195 verified-clean features + all staged additions now live on one linear LADDER — a 17-seat CORE kernel at the top, three tiers below it, a quarantine strip at the bottom, and 10 always-fed ID columns outside the ladder. A feature climbs exactly one rung, only by a pre-registered test; dishonesty kills instantly, weakness only sinks one tier.

The tier ladder — every feature has exactly one rung; movement is one rung at a time, by pre-registered test only (doctrine v1.2 · 2026-07-11)
ALWAYS FED — 10 ID COLUMNS · family, family_key, side, instrument, tf, trigger, exit, conditions, source, mech_basket — infrastructure, never tested, not a rung. side 19.4% + family 8.5% of positive importance: the model must always know what trade it is judging.
CORE — THE KERNEL (17)

The kernel — what a trader cannot take a trade without. Only 2 of 17 seats rest on doctrine rather than measurement.

FRAMEWORKAbove the weekly 21 EMA?
FRAMEWORKDistance to the weekly 21 EMA
EVIDENCEDistance to the daily 21 EMA
EVIDENCEDistance to yesterday's high
EVIDENCEDistance to yesterday's low
EVIDENCEOpening gap size
EVIDENCEPosition in today's range
EVIDENCEDistance to the opening-hour high
EVIDENCEDistance to the opening-hour low
EVIDENCEOpening-hour range width
EVIDENCEBars holding above the opening-hour high
EVIDENCEOverlap with yesterday's value
EVIDENCE20-day trend strength
EVIDENCEVolatility expanding or contracting (5d vs 20d)
EVIDENCEVIX percentile, 1-year (prior day)
EVIDENCEMinutes since the open
EVIDENCESession bucket (open-drive / midday / PM)
▲ADMISSION GATE → CORE — to climb: register the test first · prove +0.05R per trade held-out, 5 of 6 windows · pass the four trust screens (importance ≤15% · no distribution shift · coverage ≥99% · causal audit) · one clean era already served below in Tier 1 — a CORE seat is earned twice: once on lift, once on time
TIER 1 — REST OF THE TRADER MINIMUM (20)

A pro wants it; a pro could cope one bar without it. CORE + Tier 1 = the 47-column trader-minimum model — the new reference incumbent.

EVIDENCEEMA-stack detail (7)
JUDGMENTWeekly-regime persistence (1)
EVIDENCEIB remainder (5)
FRAMEWORKSwing & round-number levels (2)
JUDGMENTTrend sibling (1)
EVIDENCEVIX 5-day change (1)
EVIDENCESession clock detail (3)
▲ADMISSION GATE — to climb one rung: register the test first · prove +0.02R per trade held-out, 4 of 6 windows · pass the four trust screens · one attempt per feature per era, failures logged in the mistake ledger
TIER 2 — CONTEXT REFINERS (78)

Improves the read, doesn't create it — confluence refiners: arrival context, VWAP, pullback depth, day types, level context, lagged externals. Re-graded against the ablation evidence: v3-KEEP blocks promoted in, failed blocks demoted out.

7 · PROMOTEDArrival context (Q_LOCAL)
5 · PROMOTEDBar structure (STRUCT)
5Day-type-so-far (N_DAY)
4 · PROMOTEDRelative volume (R_VOL)
6VWAP (K)
6Pullback depth (F)
9 · JDGRally-vs-range (E)
11Yesterday's day-type flags (O_PREV)
8 · JDGHTF magnets (O_HTF)
9Overnight map (O_ON)
4 · JDGConfluence score (L_V3)
4OPEX calendar (I_OPEX)
▲ADMISSION GATE — to climb one rung: register the test first · prove +0.02R per trade held-out, 4 of 6 windows · pass the four trust screens · the holdout is never used for climbing
TIER 3 — STAGED / EXPERIMENTAL / FAILED-TO-EARN-KEEP (70 + staged adoptions)

Prove it or stay here. Nothing on this rung touches a production model until it climbs — and ALL new adoptions (the 07-11 staged batch included) enter here, none skips.

16 · ⚠ WATCHMachine-statistical (M)
8 · DEMOTEDMacro character (G)
3 · DEMOTEDConfluence count (L)
3 · DEMOTEDLevel quality (P_LEVELQ)
2 · DEMOTEDGamma context (I_GEX)
10Strategy form (META)
4Trap family (Q_TRAP)
10Sweep / premarket (SWEEP_PM)
3Seasonality (B)
4 · BLOCKEDEvent calendar (I_CAL)
3 · JDGCOT + vanna (I_COT · I_VANNA)
4 · HARD FLAGGamma walls (I_WALLS)
ENTERING 07-11 · CONDDelta block (C17, 9 subs)
ENTERING 07-11 · B8Profile bundle (C1·C2·C3·C5)
ENTERING 07-11Swing-structure machine (C14)
ENTERING 07-1107-11 staged batch (C4–C15 + SPEC)
⚠ THIEF-WATCHES–NQ magnitude family (C18, 7 subs)
ENTERING · PREREG15m 21 EMA distance + slope
DROPPED · dupPre-market relative volume (C16)
QUARANTINE — NEVER FED (receipts attached)

Burned once. Dishonesty lands here instantly — from any rung, CORE included. Re-entry needs a new mechanism + operator sign-off + a fresh climb from Tier 3.

ERA-PROXYThe clock set (6 features)
THIEFSpread z-scores
SHIFTL2 / microstructure / OFI / VPIN
LEAK · NOISESame-day externals · raw levels · 1m-derived · repainting zones · MFE-as-feature · sub-second
The rulebook — demotion & build order
Demotion — dishonesty kills, weakness sinks
  • → Quarantine instantly for dishonesty: importance share >25% (the thief pattern), availability shift in the live era, sign flips across folds, a failed causal audit, or an era-proxy verdict — no seat is above the screens, CORE included.
  • → One rung down for weakness: contributes nothing (|ΔR| < 0.005) for two straight eras — quiet features are never killed, they just lose the seat.
  • CORE demotions go to the operator with the receipt before taking effect — his master rules, he can overrule.
Build order — next model iteration
  1. CORE-only baseline — the honest floor: ID + CORE, 27 columns (<1 hr).
  2. + Tier 1 = the 47-column trader minimum — the new incumbent, priced against the frozen 179-feature v3.
  3. Tier 2 block-by-block on top, at the +0.02R gate — ~an afternoon of fits.
  4. Tier 3 climbs driven by error autopsy — only blocks that plausibly SEE what the errors share get an attempt; one per block per era.
~40–55 configs total, 2–3 working days incl. audits. Full text: FEATURE_TIER_PLAN_2026_07_11.md (Research Library → Plan & handoff).
green edge = CORE / Tier 1 (production kernel) · blue = Tier 2 refiners · teal = Tier 3 staged/experimental · grey = demoted into Tier 3 · red = blocked / quarantined · tag on every CORE seat = what the seat rests on (EVIDENCE > FRAMEWORK > JUDGMENT)
previous view (expand) — the 07-10 feature-space map + trust funnel, verbatim

~195 features in 9 baskets exist and are verified CLEAN — every feature suspected of cheating was put through the purge lab and acquitted; zero cheaters found. The frontier is what's still missing — and how "good" is judged.

Diagram B — the feature trust funnel (how a family earns its seat; judged on money, not scores)
CANDIDATE POOL
9 ranked + 4 adopted gaps open→4 TRUST TESTS
signal · stability · shift · leak→MONEY ABLATION
Δ kept-book R ≥ +0.02 · CI > 0 · 10/16 qtrs→PURGE CHECK
bullies? 12/12 acquitted→STAGED
19 families staged · 3 blocked on B8→PRODUCTION
~195 features · 9 baskets · 0 cheaters
How a feature earns its seat (judged on MONEY, not scores) candidate→ 4 trust tests (signal·stability·shift·leak)→ block ablation: Δ kept-book R ≥ +0.02, CI > 0, 10/16 quarters→ purge check (bullies?)→ frozen set
full detail (expand) — the original cards, verbatim
Have — the 9 baskets (~195, all pass the 4 trust tests)
~38Price / trendEMA regime & stack, pullback-vs-leg, 6-month character
~69Levels & locationprior D/W/M levels, IB, VWAP, level quality, overnight/premarket, confluence
~21 ⭐Arrival / trigger actionimpulse in, wick pressure, day-type-so-far — strongest basket
~16Machine / statisticalz-scores, vol-of-vol, trend-vs-chop math
~15Flows & positioninggamma, walls (2023+, ES), vanna/charm, COT, OPEX, VIX — lagged
~36Time · volume · news · identitysession clock, RVOL, FOMC/CPI proximity, strategy identity + form
Missing / next — ranked
#1 ⭐Trade economicshow big the stop is, what commissions + slippage eat as a share of that stop, how far the target sits, how much room there is to the next level — none of the 195 features cover this, and it matters: the cheapest fifth of trades lost almost nothing (−0.04R) while the most expensive fifth lost −0.76R
#2Trigger-bar mechanicshow the trigger bar itself behaved — how deep the reclaim went, how far price overshot, how hard the bar moved; entry always fills on the NEXT bar, otherwise the test cheats
#3Opportunity competitionhow many other signals fired the same direction at the same moment, how many fired against it, how crowded the moment was — about 19% of trades share exact timestamps
#4ES–NQ simultaneous staterolling-beta residual, relative momentum, leader/lag
BLOCKED · B8Volume profile (1.11 + bundle)every way of building a volume profile from 5-minute bars failed the accuracy test — the point of control came out about 10 ES points off, and even the best method fell short of the pre-committed accuracy bar. Profile features stay blocked until they are rebuilt from 1-minute data; a code hook physically prevents 5m builds [trap B8 · vp_binning.assert_vp_build_allowed()]
STAGED · S16Bond-market (ZN) momentum — lagged contextwhat the Treasury futures did yesterday, as background context for ES/NQ. The daily data is already on disk — no feed purchase needed; first job is to confirm whether an earlier probe was reading price or yield (the sign flips) [S16 · _multifactor_redo_cache/zn.parquet, 2008→2026-06, close-only · vs 2026-04-23 probe]
CANDVIX term structure (lagged)buildable from owned/free sources; prior-day values only (the same-day variant is in the graveyard)
BLOCKED · no sourceInternals (TICK/breadth) · DXY · news surprisesno sources wired; forward-only if started; macro releases = ALFRED vintages only (OPT-068)
KILLED-CLEANClock features · same-day values · repainting · depthcheating classes — caught by the trust tests
Staged 2026-07-11 — Destination A → Stage B corpus build (16 CORE, ranked)
STAGE-1 · CONDBuy-vs-sell pressure block (9 subs) C17who was hitting the bid or lifting the offer inside each bar, and whether price absorbed that pressure or gave way — the 195 features contain zero of this today; goes ahead only after the buyer/seller-flag backfill is verified and the feed-consistency test passes [trap E5]
BLOCKED · B8Volume-profile bundle C1+C2+C3+C5where the volume actually built up: how dominant the point of control is, the top-3 high-volume shelves, how value migrated from day to day, and last week's profile levels — rides the main profile build and is blocked with it until 1-minute inputs land
STAGEDMarket-structure state from confirmed swings C14are we making higher highs or lower lows, did the last new high fail, did a structure break come with real volume — a swing only counts once it is CONFIRMED, so nothing repaints after the fact; the repaint test is mandatory [trap B7]
STAGED15- and 30-minute opening-range block C12where price sits inside the opening range (thirds), whether it broke out on real volume, how many times the range got tested, and where it sits versus the pivot — all frozen the moment the window closes; the existing block only covered the first 60 minutes
STAGEDClassic floor-trader pivots C7P, R1, S1, R2, S2 computed from yesterday's regular-session high, low and close — somehow absent from all 195 features; a trivial build
STAGEDLevel crowding: touch counts + equal highs/lows C10how crowded a price area is with old highs and lows over the last 60 days — touch counts and equal extremes within a quarter of a day's range; confirmed pivots only
STAGEDA level's birth certificate C4which session created the level, on how much volume, and whether it was born in regular hours or overnight — stamped once at creation, never rewritten
STAGEDHourly grid + morning/lunch anchors C9previous hour's high/low, plus the morning high and lunch high as frozen reference targets; includes a robustness check that nudges the clock to prove nothing depends on exact minute boundaries
STAGEDBroken-range retest counter C11after the opening hour's range breaks, how many times the broken edge got retested and HELD as new support/resistance — counted on closes; rides the IB-extension card [D1/DM-04]
STAGED · merge A2cPanic/FOMO sweep-reclaim detector C13price sweeps the high or low of the last 20, 50 or 100 bars on a 2× volume spike, then reclaims it — no fixed anchor needed; duplicates a ported tool, so it is built ONCE [= port A2c]
STAGEDMonday range + weekly-extreme timing C8Monday's high and low as levels for the rest of the week, plus a running read on which weekday tends to print the week's high or low
STAGEDTime-at-price (TPO / dwell) family C6how long price SAT at each level, not how much volume traded there — acceptance measured in time; a different axis that reuses the profile plumbing
STAGEDClose-in-range position (IBS) C15where the close landed inside the bar's own high-low range — did it finish near the top or the bottom — on both the bar and the day; distinct from the body-size ratio already built
STAGED · ⚠ scarES-vs-NQ relative-state family (7 subs) C18how the two markets sit against each other: who's leading, who's lagging, whether they broke levels together, how long they've been out of sync, whose volume is running hotter. Direct descendant of the one feature that gamed the model and wrecked live results (win rate fell 63→51 out of sample), so the whole family sits on a watchlist for suspicious dominance [scar: nq_es_spread_zscore · trap C7 window-id assert]
DROPPED · dupPre-market relative volume C16dropped — the existing relative-volume feature already covers the 7:30–9:30 pre-market window (it measures cumulative volume against the same time-slot over the last 20 sessions); a pre-market-only variant would be a one-line tweak, not a build [CHECK 1 07-11 · dup of rvol_cum_tod]
STAGED batch-214 SPEC columnsS1 LVN levels (behind B8) · S2 hvn_spacing · S3 max-vol-bar levels · S4 stretch_tod_pctile · S5 td_setup_count · S6 mom_2w_skip · S7 intraday_squeeze_len · S8 GK/Parkinson vol + gk_cc_ratio (merge with port C8i) · S9 ma200min · S10 VVIX lagged · S13 rebalance_pressure · S15 turbulence_lagged · S17 vix_implied_band_pos · S18 candle_range_pct_adr
PARKEDS11 Nikkei/DAX · S12 BTC-weekend · S14 HYG/CDXall need new external feeds — parked
Staged 2026-07-10 — digest feature families (Discord mine)
BLOCKED · B81.11 prev_session_profile_levelsPOC/VAH/VAL, naked POCs, open-vs-value day-type — top build, prerequisite for DM-01 and claim D4; blocked on 5m inputs by the B8 gate verdict (1m range-uniform required)
STAGEDHow well a level has been defended 1.9a score built from: how many times the level was touched, the net buy/sell pressure at it, how deep price poked through, how sharply it bounced back, and how long price lingered — the sanctioned bar-level stand-in for the banned iceberg-detection idea; feeds the open touch-decay question [A12/X8]
STAGEDOpening-hour order · level acceptance · range-vs-VIX 1.2 · 1.8 · 1.4which end of the opening hour printed first, high or low (a directional bias clue) · how many closes in a row held beyond a level (unlocks the "80% rule" trigger) · how much of the day's range that YESTERDAY's VIX implied has already been used up (a risk input — yesterday's VIX close only, never today's)
STAGED tier-21.3 ib_rotation_count · 1.21 news_candle_levels · 1.15 delta_dryup_at_extreme1.21 = prerequisite for DM-03; 1.15 pending the delta-backfill check
PRIORITY ↑1.5 esnq_sync · 1.6 esnq_sweep_smt · 1.17 mtf_trend_conflictalready-planned items with raised build priority (bet-#4 ES-NQ / multi-TF classes)
Adopted gaps 2026-07-11 — new feature families (from the 217-option review)
ADOPTED-GAPOPT-019 futures curve/basis/carry familyPIT curve slope, carry, basis convergence, roll yield, OI migration from raw dated ES/NQ contracts with causal rolls (=OPT-059, =OPT-216/XA-7) — own-instrument, genuinely absent context family; trap B3 builds half the plumbing → Stage B + one claims-queue verify
ADOPTED-GAPOPT-044 CRT candle-range relationshipssmall fixed categorical feature family, adjacent to staged S18/C15 but not identical → Stage B batch-2 / bet-#4 primitive
ADOPTED-GAPOPT-066 path signatures / intrabar shaperough-path summaries vs cheap handcrafted path stats (C17 precedent sanctions tick-derived bar-level features); must beat handcrafted baselines to earn a seat → Stage B batch-2 / bet-#4 primitive
PARTIAL residueOPT-052 trade-sign persistence / order-splittingtrade-sign autocorrelation / run-length from the same aggressor feed — rides the C17 backfill at near-zero marginal cost
Law: killed for dishonesty, never weakness — but "weak-and-trustworthy" no longer auto-ships: the Kronos embeddings were trustworthy AND real, still hurt (dilution). Money-lift decides.

Phase 3 — LABEL

Same trades + different label = a different skill learned. Proven the project's biggest lever.

The label decision tree — same trades, different definition of “good” = a different skill learned
WHAT DOES
“GOOD” MEAN?
TESTED —
VERDICTS IN
CHAMPIONWinner/loser terciles
OVERTURNEDFirst-touch (+1R before −1R)
KILLED-CLEANBinary win/loss
KILLED-CLEANR regression
KILLED-CLEAN as targetMFE/MAE
UNTESTED
MENU
NEW #1 ⭐Absolute action value Q(x, a)
CANDTriple-barrier + meta-labeling
CANDRanking · distributional · multi-horizon
CANDBenchmark-aligned
FOLLOW-UPEnsemble of labels
red struck-through leaves = closed questions in the graveyard · grey = overturned · amber = next up
full detail (expand) — the original cards, verbatim
Tested — verdicts in
CHAMPIONWinner/loser tercilesthe only definition of "good trade" the model could reliably learn across time periods — but it ranks trades AGAINST EACH OTHER: in 14 of 31 strategy families the "good" bucket is just a smaller loss
OVERTURNEDFirst-touch (+1R before −1R)cleanly anchored it's barely learnable; early appeal was label contamination
KILLED-CLEANBinary win/lossunlearnable — coin flip
KILLED-CLEANR regressionlearnable, never profitable
KILLED-CLEAN as targetMFE/MAEMOST learnable, WORST money — selects volatility. Kept as forensic tool
Open menu — untested
NEW #1 ⭐Absolute action value Q(x, a)teach the model the expected NET R of each choice — long, short, or stand aside — and only take a trade when even the cautious low-end estimate is still positive after costs
CANDTriple-barrier + meta-labelingtextbook institutional method, never tried here
CANDRanking · distributional · multi-horizonorder the day's fires / full outcome range / per-horizon
CANDBenchmark-alignedlabel = contribution to P(funded ≤ 10d)
FOLLOW-UPEnsemble of labelsproven: different labels pick near-disjoint books
The label gauntlet pre-committed winner rule→ identical features/splits per arm→ judged on kept-book MONEY, never scores→ shuffle control + fold-majority
The MFE lesson (sticky): any label rewarding "how far price CAN run" secretly selects volatility — scores high, loses money. Learnability ≠ profitability.

Phase 4 — MODEL

Least important phase by design (data > features > label > model) — but era 2 adds a second job: not just judging fires, MINTING rules.

Diagram A — judging (filter era): dataset → champion, challengers alongside, killed lanes severed
V5 DATASET
fires × features × label
CHAMPIONCatBoost
PHASE 5 — USAGE
challenger lane — must beat the champion on identical data
OPENPer-family / per-TF / hierarchical heads
OPENCalibration layer · tree ensembles
ADOPTED-GAPOPT-097 TabPFN / small-data tabular prior
PARTIAL residueOPT-009 local-vs-pooled ablation
ADOPTED-GAP · guardedOPT-093 train-fold-only synthetic augmentation
KILLED-CLEAN 16/16Kronos fine-tune
KILLED-CLEAN · demotedText-LLM fine-tune
red ✕ = dead-end stub, lane killed clean · challengers replace the champion only by beating it on identical folds
Diagram B — minting (strategy-creation era): ranked alternatives converge on one gauntlet
ALL-BAR ACTION CORPUS
(era 2, from Phase 1)
#1 ⭐Policy-tree / rule-list mining
#2Tree-path extraction
#3Conditioned grid over NEW primitives
#4Capped symbolic / genetic search
LASTSequence-model proposals
ANTI-FOOLING GAUNTLET
→ freeze 1–3 rules max
the ranked routes are real alternatives — whichever mints candidates, only the gauntlet's 1–3 survivors freeze
full detail (expand) — the original cards, verbatim
Judging (filter era)
CHAMPIONCatBoostthe reigning model (a gradient-boosted decision-tree engine) — it passed the scrambled-label honesty test, and anything new must beat it on identical data
OPENPer-family / per-TF / hierarchical headspartial pooling for thin families; family baseline as model offset
OPENCalibration layer · tree ensembleshonest probabilities; CatBoost+LGBM+XGB agreement
ADOPTED-GAPOPT-097 TabPFN / small-data tabular priorcheap one-shot opponent for the CatBoost champion, benchmarked under identical PIT folds — either loses fast or flags an easy upgrade
PARTIAL residueOPT-009 local-vs-pooled ablationexplicit local vs pooled vs leave-one-contract-out with asset intercepts + unrelated-pool negative control (=OPT-105/188/200/213) — one cheap experiment on the shared ES/NQ model
ADOPTED-GAP · guardedOPT-093 train-fold-only synthetic augmentationgenerative augmentation/stress in TRAINING folds only, adopted only with the OPT-139 synthetic-data protocol attached verbatim (never in validation — OPT-154); low priority, block-bootstrap already covers bet-#3 stress (=OPT-208/TS-6)
KILLED-CLEAN 16/16Kronos fine-tunefrozen-embedding gate failed decisively; deep lane parked
KILLED-CLEAN · demotedText-LLM fine-tunetext pretraining doesn't transfer to markets
Minting (strategy-creation era) — ranked
#1 ⭐Policy-tree / rule-list mininglet a shallow, human-readable decision tree learn when to go long, short or stay flat on the all-bar corpus — rules capped at 2-4 conditions so a trader can read every rule it writes; it only trades when the cautious estimate of edge is positive
#2Tree-path extractiondistill ensemble paths into mechanical triggers
#3Conditioned grid over NEW primitivestrigger-bar / multi-TF / ES-NQ primitives only, FDR-controlled
#4Capped symbolic / genetic searchmax 4-5 primitives — bounded, never unlimited noise search
LASTSequence-model proposalsKronos already reduced this prior; proposal-only if ever
The anti-fooling discipline for mining (thousands of candidates) pre-declared universe→ nested chronology (re-mine inside every fold)→ FDR on exploration→ full-pipeline null test on the ONE winner→ freeze 1–3 rules max
Laws: beat the champion on identical data · complexity is the last lever · too-good = bug first (every spectacular PF in project history was a bug).

Phase 5 — USAGE

What execution does with a signal: veto or size, which timeframes, and the account policy. The phase the second-opinion review changed most.

The usage stack — a signal falls through these layers, top to bottom
Signal (from phases 1–4)
strategy fire + judge score
Trade layer — take it, veto it, route it
DEMOTEDVeto filter
DECIDEDPer-timeframe routing
CANDSizing by score · multi-judge voting · regime thresholds
REQUIREDJudge-in-loop replay
KILLED-CLEANExit optimization · re-entry capture
Sizing layer — how big, given the floor room left
PROMOTED ⭐Sizing / reset policy (Bellman)
STAGED #1Worst-case drawdown sizing calibrator S3-14
STAGEDProtect a green day · the core size formula ATOM-15 · ATOM-16
STAGEDVolatility size cap · losing-streak survival ATOM-17 · ATOM-18
ADOPTED-GAPOPT-100 empirical-Bayes shrinkage
PARTIAL residueOPT-103 CVaR / robust-objective ablation arm
Account policy layer — the bet-#3 atoms, clustered
eval objective & pacing
STAGED #2S3-01 eval objective
STAGEDS3-02/13/15/17/18 + SPEC S3-23/24/26/27/28
DD engine & the $2k floor
STAGEDS3-07 DD-engine taxonomy + S3-19 limit-consequence semantics
STAGEDS3-04/05/06 withdrawal-buffer / staged unlock / floor halt · S3-12 day-PnL trailing lock
STAGEDDaily loss stop ATOM-11
consistency / day rules
STAGEDATOM-12 daily_profit_stop · ATOM-13 loss_count_stop · ATOM-14 trade_count_cap
STAGEDS3-08/09/11 consistency-rule matrix + scheduler + band
lifecycle & payouts
STAGED · baseS3-03 lifecycle switch at first payout
STAGEDS3-10 min-days probe + S3-19 activity probe
STAGEDATOM-25 prop_routing · ATOM-26 payout_pacer · ATOM-27 account_partials · ATOM-33 cooldowns
lint & guards
STAGED · lintATOM-24 martingale_ban
STAGED · lintS3-20 sim-first launch gate · S3-21 flat-size lint · S3-29 negative control
GUARDZero-edge control
Fleet layer — many accounts
STAGED #3Spread strategies across accounts S3-16
CANDParallel accounts
ADOPTED-GAPOPT-008 joint trade/order netting across sleeves
Routed elsewhere / parked (not in this stack)
RE-ROUTEDATOM-32 stop_placement → bet #2 · ATOM-28/29/30 → ops standards
RE-ROUTEDS3-25 copier hard-caps → ops standards
PARKEDS3-22 eval-only strategy routing
each band only sees what the band above lets through; the dashed box holds atoms re-routed to other bets or parked
full detail (expand) — the original cards, verbatim
Trade-level
DEMOTEDVeto filterhalves losses but capped: profitable only at tiny keep-rates. New role: secondary gate over already-positive strategies
DECIDEDPer-timeframe routing5m + 15m only; 1-minute excluded (no edge, 5y proof)
CANDSizing by score · multi-judge voting · regime thresholdsafter something is positive; disjoint-books fact supports voting
REQUIREDJudge-in-loop replayvetoes free position slots → later trades change; static replay ≠ live counterfactual
KILLED-CLEANExit optimization · re-entry captureboth proven dead (originals near-optimal; excursion = volatility mirage)
Account-level (the benchmark layer)
PROMOTED ⭐Sizing / reset policy (Bellman)optimal risk-per-trade as % of remaining floor room under the $2k trail; resets valued as actions; now also risk-forecast-aware (vol-first sizing adopted from the research review)
CANDParallel accountsonly if genuinely independent — independence measured, never assumed
GUARDZero-edge controlevery policy must beat shuffled/no-edge paths under identical rules — a coin flip + bold resets "passes" 94% (lottery, not alpha)
Bet #3 action space — the 13 digest atoms (adopted 07-10; Destination C)
STAGEDDaily loss stop ATOM-11cap the day's loss at roughly one-sixth to one-seventh of the total drawdown allowance (about $285–333 on the $2k trail), tested head-to-head against the current day-stop rule
STAGEDATOM-12 daily_profit_stop · ATOM-13 loss_count_stop · ATOM-14 trade_count_cappayout-pacing stop · CB(2L) family (pairs with C009 cooldown) · per-day trade cap
STAGEDProtect a green day · the core size formula ATOM-15 · ATOM-16once the day is green, risk less so you keep it · CORE rule: size every trade as a % (5–10%) of the drawdown room you have LEFT under the trailing floor — never from the account balance
STAGEDVolatility size cap · losing-streak survival ATOM-17 · ATOM-18cut size when volatility spikes (matches the adopted vol-first sizing) · size so the account survives 34+ losses in a row, and halt trading if a streak runs one worse than anything seen in backtest
STAGED · lintATOM-24 martingale_banhard lint on the policy search space
STAGEDATOM-25 prop_routing · ATOM-26 payout_pacer · ATOM-27 account_partials · ATOM-33 cooldownseval/funded asymmetry · 5×$200 winning days + consistency cap + qualifying lock · close k/N accounts at TP1 · 10–30 min post-trade spacing
RE-ROUTEDATOM-32 stop_placement → bet #2 · ATOM-28/29/30 → ops standardsstop-anchor menu moved to the cost-geometry race; ops atoms incl. the "emulated limits release as MARKET orders" costing rule; rolling monthly-cohort pass-rate adopted as a bet-#3 report metric
Bet #3 action space — the 27 triage S3 atoms (adopted 07-11; Destination C)
STAGED #1Worst-case drawdown sizing calibrator S3-14find the biggest size at which even a 95th-percentile bad run of losses (resampled from real trade history) still fits inside the drawdown room left on the trail — makes deploy-gate 3 a formula instead of a judgment call [pairs with trap D4]
STAGED #2S3-01 eval objectiveP(pass) × time × reset-cost with fee terms — the objective function itself
STAGED #3Spread strategies across accounts S3-16route different strategies (or rotating subsets) to different accounts so they don't all draw down together — the cheapest way to raise the odds that at least ONE account gets funded without needing more edge; also model the extra slippage when many accounts want the same fill
STAGEDS3-07 DD-engine taxonomy + S3-19 limit-consequence semanticsDD-basis enum, soft-breach vs death, firm action-space table — load-bearing for multi-firm math (with traps D1/D2)
STAGEDS3-08/09/11 consistency-rule matrix + scheduler + bandpayout-shape constraints as first-class scheduling
STAGED · baseS3-03 lifecycle switch at first payoutstate discontinuity; the drain-and-reset cycling sub-variant is TOS-gray → DECISION-NEEDED, out of the simulator until ruled
STAGEDS3-04/05/06 withdrawal-buffer / staged unlock / floor halt · S3-12 day-PnL trailing lockaction menu around the $2k floor · account-layer giveback stop (P1 precedent, NOT the killed per-trade trail)
STAGEDS3-10 min-days probe + S3-19 activity probecompliance actions on the 10-day clock
STAGED · lintS3-20 sim-first launch gate · S3-21 flat-size lint · S3-29 negative controleval-lint layer; S3-29 = profit-target-no-loss-stop as a must-fail fixture
STAGEDS3-02/13/15/17/18 + SPEC S3-23/24/26/27/28competing eval-pace arm, cushion ladder, param addenda, event routing, purchase economics + de-risk step, launch timing, probe calibration, bracket lint, hold-time matrix; S3-18 Topstep throttle / DDL-double-payout = UNVERIFIED room intel → folded into the "verify Topstep terms" operator item
PARKEDS3-22 eval-only strategy routingonly if the simulator shows eval EV is strategy-limited
RE-ROUTEDS3-25 copier hard-caps → ops standardserror-rate knob stays in trap D3
Bet #3 — adopted gaps 2026-07-11
ADOPTED-GAPOPT-100 empirical-Bayes shrinkageshrink noisy per-strategy / per-regime / per-instrument edge estimates before the simulator consumes them — the cheapest fix for small-N sleeves
PARTIAL residueOPT-103 CVaR / robust-objective ablation armmean-variance vs downside/CVaR/robust utility under identical constraints — one cheap ablation (=OPT-190, =OPT-217/XA-8)
ADOPTED-GAPOPT-008 joint trade/order netting across sleevesnet opposing intended orders before execution, schedule residual only (=OPT-194/XS-4) — free turnover/cost reduction once ≥2 sleeves run per account; irrelevant today, load-bearing at fleet scale (with ATOM-28/29/30)
The 5-day math (honest expectation table) $150 × 3/day: 5-day pass IMPOSSIBLE· E[profit] = target → P5 only ~55-62%· ~95% needs ≈6.5–7R/day (e.g. 70% WR · +2R · 6/day, net)
Law: "Policy can buy passage probability; it cannot certify alpha."

Phase 7 — ROAD TO LIVE

The gated sequence between "great backtest" and real money. Mostly linear — the options live upstream. (Numbering note: phases jump 5 → 7 by historical accident; there never was a Phase 6 — the gap is cosmetic, nothing is hidden.)

The gates, left to right — genuinely linear; no strategy skips a step
NOW ▸ rehearsal — staging & falsifier prep, pre-gate
GATE 1Falsifier stackshuffled-label refits, drop-best-quarter, 1.5× cost stress — survive all
GATE 2 ⚠TRUE vault, opened onceuntouched data growing daily since 2026-06-05; min ~84 trading days (~Oct earliest); the old Q2 vault = design-touched robustness check only
GATE 3Shadow / paperfrozen config on live fires, no money; exact feature/threshold/order parity required
GATE 4The 4 promotion gatessolo-pass · decorrelated · fits the $2k trail · LIFTS P(funded ≤ 10d)
GATE 5Livewith drift monitoring, retrain policy, kill-switch criteria
REAL MONEY
dashed final connector: live is reached only with drift monitoring, retrain policy and kill-switch criteria armed
full detail (expand) — the original cards, verbatim
The gates, in order
GATE 1Falsifier stackshuffled-label refits, drop-best-quarter, 1.5× cost stress — survive all
GATE 2 ⚠TRUE vault, opened onceuntouched data growing daily since 2026-06-05; min ~84 trading days (~Oct earliest); the old Q2 vault = design-touched robustness check only
GATE 3Shadow / paperfrozen config on live fires, no money; exact feature/threshold/order parity required
GATE 4The 4 promotion gatessolo-pass · decorrelated · fits the $2k trail · LIFTS P(funded ≤ 10d)
GATE 5Livewith drift monitoring, retrain policy, kill-switch criteria
Law: forward performance is the only real judge — everything before gate 3 is rehearsal. After the vault read: no rescue tuning, ever.

Setups To Test — new trade ideas headed for the sweep (dest B · staged 2026-07-10/11)

New surface: mechanism candidates headed for the operator-managed trigger-lab sweep terminal (separate terminal; feeds bets 2/4 and the Stage-B corpus — Stage B waits on sweep results). Every candidate ends at SHADOW-READY; no HYDRA wiring. Sources: SUGGESTIONS_TRIAGE.md §3, DISCORD_MINE_DIGEST §3, CONDITIONAL_CHECKS_2026_07_11.md.

The trapped breakdown of the hour candle STAGE-1 M-03 · TRAP/CARRY · = port A3/A3b

Freeze the high, low and midpoint of the first half of a 60/120/240-minute candle. When price later breaks below that first-half low, finds no sellers, and a 5/15m bar closes back above both the low and the midpoint, the breakdown sellers are trapped — buy that close (or rest a limit at the midpoint). Stop at the running low of the big candle, first target the opposite first-half extreme.

full playbook entry + spec

Pick an anchor candle on a higher timeframe — 60, 120 or 240 minutes. The moment its first half completes, freeze three prices: the first-half high, the first-half low, and the midpoint between them. Now watch for a trap: price breaks BELOW the first-half low later in that candle, the breakdown finds no sellers, and a 5- or 15-minute bar closes back above both the first-half low and the midpoint. The sellers who chased that breakdown are now trapped — you buy the qualifying close (or rest a limit at the midpoint). The stop sits at the running low of the hour candle, which is naturally wide — roughly half the candle's range — so the trade sizes small and survives noise. First target is the opposite first-half extreme, then the standard exit menu. One signal per anchor candle, confirmed on the execution bar's close, nothing repaints because the first-half values are frozen. On ES the 60-minute half-range usually sits under the 12.5-point cost floor, so ES runs the 120/240-minute arms only. This is the only mined setup where every number came straight from working source code; it gets built ONCE together with its ported twin (the Candle Majick decomposition in the Ported Tools drawer). A companion CARRY arm (close above the first-half high with close-location ≥ 0.70 and a bullish VWAP regime) rides along. Before anything is believed, it must prove it is not just the live failed-breakout strategies wearing a different anchor.

entry: 5m/15m close > fhL AND > fhM after a break below fhL (or limit at fhM) · stop: running HTF-candle low · target: opposite first-half extreme → standard menu · params from source: continuationClosePct 0.70, vwapSlopeLen 5, vwapFlatTicks 2.0, one signal per HTF candle · anchors: 60/120/240m (ES: 120/240m only) · session: RTH exec bars · status: STAGE-1 — decorr check vs failed_breakdown, F08/F26/C1 at gate

The morning after a hard down-trend day STAGE-1 M-01 · classifier → C052

After a strict trend day straight down (price never re-crossed VWAP after 10:30), sellers are usually spent — those days almost never chain back-to-back. Next morning, once a new low prints below the open, the first 5/15m close back above the prior bar's high is the buy. Stop under the session low with a buffer, resting target at the session VWAP.

full playbook entry + spec

Yesterday was a strict trend day straight down — by the mechanical definition, price never re-crossed the session VWAP after 10:30 ET. Statistically those days almost never chain back-to-back: in twelve years the source counts exactly two back-to-back downside pairs on NQ and one on ES. The forced selling is spent. So the next morning you look for the first mechanical sign the sellers are done: after a new session low prints below the open, the first 5- or 15-minute close back above the prior bar's high is your buy trigger (a stricter arm waits until the open-to-low drop has already stretched at least half a daily ATR). Stop goes under the session low with a quarter-ATR buffer — wide on purpose, because the day after a trend day is a high-volatility day, and you size for that. Target is a resting limit at the session VWAP; an optional runner goes to VWAP plus one standard deviation. Entries 10:00–15:30 ET only, flat by the close. The rarity stat gets verified first on our own data — it costs nothing — and even if the trade arm dies, the trend-day classifier survives as a feature and as the kill-switch input.

condition: prior RTH day never re-crossed VWAP after 10:30 ET · entry: first 5m/15m close > prior bar high after a new low below the open (arm B: only after open-to-low ≥ k×dailyATR, k ∈ {0.5, 0.75, 1.0}) · stop: session LOD − 0.25×ATR(5m) · target: resting limit at RTH VWAP (runner: VWAP+1σ) · session: RTH 10:00–15:30, EOD flat · status: STAGE-1 — b2b-rarity stat batch-verified first, free

The inside bar after a stop run STAGED M-02

Stops get run through an obvious level (day, overnight, or prior-day extreme) and price closes back inside — the sweep failed. Wait for the first inside bar to coil near the sweep, then enter on a stop order through it in the reclaim direction. Stop beyond the sweep extreme, target the prior session's POC or the next level.

full playbook entry + spec

Price runs the stops through an obvious level — the day's low or high, the overnight extreme, or yesterday's — and closes back inside within a bar or three. The sweep failed. Now you wait for the market to coil: the first inside bar (a bar whose whole range sits inside the previous bar's) forming within a few bars of the sweep. You place a stop order just through that inside bar in the reclaim direction — above its high after a downside sweep, below its low after an upside sweep — and cancel it if nothing triggers within 2–4 bars. The stop goes beyond the sweep extreme with a quarter-ATR buffer: wide, honest, and exactly where the trade is proven wrong. Target is the prior session's point of control (once the profile build unblocks) or the next session level in your direction, with a proportional fallback. The sessions the source actually traded: the power hour (15:00–16:00 ET) after a midday sweep, plus an overnight variant that runs as its own arm. One caution stands: the live book already runs an inside-bar strategy, so this must prove it wins and loses on different days before promotion.

sweep: trade through marked level (LOD/HOD, ON H/L, prior-day H/L), close back inside within N ∈ {1,2,3} bars · setup: first inside bar within M ∈ {3..8} bars of sweep · entry: stop order at inside-bar high/low, cancel after 2–4 bars · stop: sweep extreme ± 0.25×ATR · target: prior-session POC (needs 1.11) or next level; proportional tp_r fallback · sessions: power hour after 12:00–13:30 sweep; ETH variant as arm · status: STAGED — decorr vs live inside_bar at gate

Fade the outer VWAP band — balanced days only STAGE-2 M-06 · vs F03/C4

On a balanced day — price living inside the VWAP bands — a rejection at the 2σ band (wick beyond, close back inside) is a fade back toward VWAP. Stop beyond the rejection extreme, target VWAP itself. The moment two straight 5m bars CLOSE beyond the band the day is migrating: cancel everything and stand down.

full playbook entry + spec

First classify the day, then trade it. As long as price lives inside the VWAP bands the day is "in balance", and a rejection at the outer two-standard-deviation band — a wick beyond it with a close back inside — is a fade back toward VWAP. The moment two consecutive 5-minute bars CLOSE beyond that outer band, the day has flipped to "migrating": the market wants a new fair price, you cancel everything and stand down until price re-enters the inner band. Entries only 10:00–15:30 ET, skipping the opening half-hour while the bands are still forming. Stop beyond the rejection extreme, at minimum beyond the band plus a quarter-ATR. Target is VWAP itself, with the one-sigma band as a partial. The kill condition was agreed before the test: if its daily P&L just mirrors the two live VWAP strategies, it dies — but the balance-or-migrating state read survives as a feature either way. Never trade the VWAP centerline itself; the plain band fade without the state machine is already rejected.

state: balance = price inside bands · migration kill: 2 consecutive 5m closes beyond 2σ → stand down until back inside 1σ · entry: 5m rejection bar at ±2σ (wick beyond, close inside; 1σ weak arm), fade toward VWAP · stop: beyond rejection extreme, min 2σ band + 0.25×ATR · target: VWAP (1σ partial) · session: 10:00–15:30 ET · status: STAGE-2 — pre-registered kill: collapse onto F03/C4 daily-R corr ⇒ dead; state feature survives

Opening range measured in volume, not minutes STAGE-2 M-04 · scope shelf OPT-067

Instead of freezing the opening range at a clock time, freeze it once enough volume has traded — narrow and early on a fast tape, auto-wider on a slow one. Then it's the standard break play: enter on a 5m close beyond the frozen range, stop at the opposite extreme plus a buffer, proportional target off the range size.

full playbook entry + spec

Everyone freezes the opening range at a clock time; this version freezes it when enough BUSINESS has been done — the range locks at the first bar where cumulative session volume crosses a threshold. On a fast tape the range completes early and comes out narrower, giving earlier and cleaner breaks; on a slow day it auto-widens. After the freeze it is the standard opening-range break play: enter on a 5-minute close beyond the range, stop at the opposite extreme plus a buffer, proportional target off the range size. The community shipped the construction but never disclosed their entry, stop or target — those are set by the sweep; the volume thresholds run as arms at 25%, 40% and 60% of the trailing 20-day median first-hour volume. This is the one sanctioned experiment with volume-defined bars — extending the idea any further is a constitution change that needs an operator ruling.

range: OR H/L frozen at first bar where cum RTH volume ≥ V, V ∈ {25%, 40%, 60%} of 20d median first-hour volume · entry: 5m close beyond frozen range · stop: opposite range extreme + buffer, floor-skip · target: proportional tp_r × range · status: STAGE-2 — one-knob anchor swap on existing or_break machinery; entry/stop/target set by the sweep

The 18:00 reopen and 15:00 opening ranges UNBLOCKED · whitelist M-05 · DECISION-NEEDED

Draw an opening range off the first 5 minutes of the 18:00 ET futures reopen (and separately at 15:00). On a 5m close beyond it, don't chase — rest a limit at the broken edge for the retest, stop beyond the opposite boundary. Skip thin sessions (range under 20 NQ / 12.5 ES points); nobody in our corpus trades this hour, so it's decorrelated by construction.

full playbook entry + spec

Nobody in our corpus trades the 18:00 ET futures reopen at all — which makes any structure there decorrelated from the entire RTH book by construction. The play: draw the opening range off the first 5 minutes (10- and 15-minute arms) at the 18:00 reopen, and separately at 15:00 ET. When a 5-minute bar closes beyond the range, don't chase — rest a limit at the broken edge and wait for the retest tap, cancelling if it doesn't fill within 3–6 bars. Stop beyond the opposite boundary. The reopen ranges are often thin, so the cost law becomes the entry gate itself: skip any session where the range is under 20 NQ / 12.5 ES points. The community took profit at half the range — a fat-stop, thin-target shape that needs a very high win rate — so the sweep runs 50%, 100% and proportional target arms and judges on net dollar expectancy. The data trap that killed CCM-FVG was checked first: the default DEV data is overnight-blind (0–13% coverage), so this may ONLY run on the whitelisted 24-hour sources. Whether an overnight session belongs in the program at all is still the operator's call.

range: first 5m bar (arms 10/15m) at 18:00 ET reopen + separately 15:00 ET · trigger: 5m close beyond range (1m origin quarantined — killed TF) · entry: resting limit at broken edge, cancel 3–6 bars; VWAP-anchored limit arm · stop: beyond opposite boundary + buffer · min-range gate: skip if range < 20 NQ / 12.5 ES pt · target arms: 50% / 100% / tp_r-proportional · data: whitelisted 24h sources ONLY (databento 1m 2016-2024 · nq_1min_from_ticks · ohlcv_5m_24h · HUD ≥2026-06); cache/ohlcv_5m/ + ohlcv_1m DEV = invalid by construction · status: coverage CHECK 2 PASS 07-11; overnight SCOPE = operator call
Battery arms from the Discord digest — feed-to-sweep, run order re-ranked

The approach-velocity gate — runs first STAGED · run 1st DM-02

Not a new trade — a question asked of trades we already took. Replay the passive-limit fill logs and cancel, on paper, every entry where price was rushing into the level over the last three 1-minute bars; if the kept fills beat a random-cancel placebo at the same skip rate, the gate is real. Cheapest test in the package.

full playbook entry + spec

Not a new trade — a question asked of trades we already took. Replay the existing passive-limit fill logs and cancel, on paper, every entry where price was rushing into the level over the last three 1-minute bars ("would pull bids if we start to flash dump" — the live rule this mechanizes). If the fills you kept were meaningfully better than the ones a random-cancel placebo keeps at the same skip rate, the gate is real. Cheapest test in the package and the most information per hour; a 5-minute-computed sibling runs as an arm, and a second ablation checks fills with a fresh liquidity sweep behind them versus without.

form: pure ablation on existing passive-limit fills — no new trades, no cost exposure · gate: approach speed over last 3×1m bars (5m sibling arm) · control: random-cancel placebo at equal skip-rate · status: STAGED, runs FIRST

The initial-balance extension fade STAGED DM-04 → merged into expansion-D1

When price runs to 1.2× beyond the first hour's range (the initial balance), the move is usually stretched — fade it back toward the range. Stop out at 1.75× the extension, first target a 25% retracement back into the IB. Roughly 45–80 NQ points of risk, so NQ clears the cost floor easily.

full playbook entry + spec

When the first hour's range (the initial balance) gets extended far beyond itself, the move is usually stretched: enter the fade when price has run to 1.2× the IB extension (1.4× as a second arm), with the stop out at 1.75× — about 0.55 of the IB range, roughly 45–80 NQ points or 11–22 ES points, so NQ passes the cost floor easily and ES sits near it. The community's own numbers enrich the card: stops 15–20 NQ points beyond the pullback extreme ("not 10 — NQ needs room"), first target 25% retracement back into the range, a fade-quality prior ("the deeper it already ran, the more a pullback to the IB high is a top, not a re-entry"), and a VWAP+2σ gate. Built once with the D1 Dalton IB plays — the best geometry in the batch and literally the bet-#2 thesis.

entry: 1.2×IB-extension tag (1.4 arm) · stop: 1.75×ext ⇒ ≈0.55×IB-range ≈ 45–80 NQ pt / 11–22 ES pt (floors applied) · community rows: SL 15–20 NQ pt beyond pullback extreme · TP1 25% retrace into IB · VWAP+2σ gate · C-period close-outside-IB confirm · status: STAGED — one build with D1

The prior-POC timeout inversion BLOCKED · via B8 DM-01

Most mornings price comes back early to touch yesterday's point of control. When the touch DOESN'T come by a deadline, that refusal means the market is accepting the new prices — trade the inversion. Stop at the opposite extreme of the 10:00–10:15 range; blocked until the 1-minute profile rebuild lands.

full playbook entry + spec

Most mornings, price goes back and touches yesterday's point of control early. When the touch DOESN'T come by a deadline, that refusal is information — the market is accepting the new prices, and you trade the inversion. The stop is the opposite extreme of the 10:00–10:15 range, capped at 1.5 daily-ATR-scaled 5-minute ATRs, with the 12.5/20-point floors enforced explicitly. The timeout deadline itself isn't guessed — claim B4 in the verification queue derives it from data. The whole trade waits on the volume-profile rebuild: the B8 trap proved profiles built from 5-minute bars put the POC about 10 ES points off, so this is blocked until 1-minute inputs land. The card carries the standing caution: the base rates are real, but naive touch-monetization is dead — judge conditional expectancy against adverse excursion, not touch rates.

condition: prior-day POC untouched by timeout T (T from claim S6-B4) · stop: opposite extreme of 10:00–10:15 range, cap 1.5×ATR(5m), floors enforced · prerequisite: 1.11 profile build (1m range-uniform inputs — B8 gate) · status: BLOCKED behind B8; scheduled after 1.11

The ES/NQ leader-laggard pairs sleeve STAGED C010-B

At 12:00, read which of ES and NQ is leading and put the two legs on against each other, flat by the close — profits don't depend on market direction at all, which is why it earned a slot. Disaster stop if the spread blows out 3σ; the honest killer is double the costs on two legs.

full playbook entry + spec

The one candidate in the whole package whose profits would not depend on market direction at all: at 12:00, read which of ES and NQ is leading, put the two legs on against each other, and go flat by the close, with a disaster stop if the spread blows out three standard deviations. Zero-beta means it diversifies the book by construction — the reason it earned a slot. The honest killer is stated up front: two legs means double the market costs (and micro contracts make the per-unit fees worse), so a 2× cost wall is expected. Its statistics stay blocked by the pairs trap until the accounting treats a pair as ONE trade, never two.

signal: 12:00 leader/laggard read · legs: ES vs NQ, EOD flat · disaster stop: spread +3σ · risk quantified vs the $2k floor · expected wall: 2× two-leg costs · status: STAGED — trap C8 (pairs-legs-are-not-trades) blocks its stats until wired

The event-candle midpoint retest DECISION-NEEDED DM-03

A scheduled release prints an impulse candle at least 3× the pre-event ATR; the 50% midpoint of that candle becomes a level, and the retest of it is the trade. Stop at the impulse candle's opposite extreme. Any test must use 1-minute delayed fills and doubled costs, because at event time the spread blows out.

full playbook entry + spec

After a scheduled release prints an impulse candle at least three times the pre-event ATR, the 50% midpoint of that candle becomes a level, and the retest of it is the trade. The stop is the impulse candle's opposite extreme — likely wide enough — but the true killer at event time is the spread blowing out, so the spec mandates 1-minute delayed fills and doubled costs in any test. Whether this runs at all is not our call: the event/calendar mechanism lane was closed with six honest NULLs, and the operator must rule whether release-bar level construction is a genuinely new mechanism class. The prerequisite feature (news-candle levels, 1.21) is staged either way; the statistics get verified regardless.

level: 50% midpoint of release impulse candle (≥3× pre-event ATR) · entry: retest of midpoint · stop: impulse-candle opposite extreme · mandatory: M1 delayed fills + doubled event-window costs · prerequisite: feature 1.21 · status: CONDITIONAL on operator adjudication of the event-lane 6-NULLs receipt; runs last of the accepted arms
Param enrichment + standing pointers

Community parameter rows onto already-staged cards STAGED · rows

The mine also produced numbers the community actually traded with, loaded as parameter rows onto cards that already exist — the IB plays (D1/DM-04), the turtle-soup Donchian card (a 2-day-lookback arm entered on a close below the prior 2-day low, gated by a rising 50-day EMA), the POC timeout, the velocity gate, and the break-of-structure retest family. The best of it is four gate atoms for bos_retest — suppress entries after a lower-high, a proximity veto, a bar-body veto, and zone expiry — going onto X10–X13, four of our biggest-sample families which today carry zero condition atoms.

The trigger-lab re-sweep is already running ACTIVE

The operator manages the sweep in a separate terminal out of results/trigger_lab_autonomous_2026_07_11/. Everything above feeds it; Stage B corpus assembly waits on its results (PLAN §9).

Twelve ideas the supplier rejected itself KILLED-CLEAN

The mine's own curator threw out twelve of its mechanisms before we ever saw them — a same-hour collision play, a CPI straddle, a 5-point-stop gamma bracket, the plain VWAP band fade without the state machine, and the rest. We checked the reasoning and endorsed every call; the receipts live in the Graveyard drawer.

original cards (expand) — preserved verbatim
The 5+1 staged mechanisms
STAGE-1Failed breakdown of the first-half range M-03 TRAP/CARRYprice breaks below the frozen range of the session's first half, the breakdown fails, and you buy back at the range midpoint with the stop at the running low — the only mined setup where every number came straight from the source; built ONCE together with its ported twin; ES needs 2h/4h variants; must prove it isn't just the live failed-breakout strategies in disguise [= port A3/A3b · decorr vs failed_breakdown, F08/F26/C1]
STAGE-1Day after a hard down-trend day → buy back to VWAP M-01the session after a strict downside trend day, buy the first reclaim, stop below the low of day, target the session VWAP; first check the free stat on how rarely two such days print back-to-back — and even if the trade dies, the trend-day classifier survives as a feature [→ C052]
STAGEDM-02 post-sweep inside-bar breaksweep+reclaim → inside bar → stop-through entry; stop beyond sweep extreme, target POC/next level; live book runs inside_bar — decorrelation check at gate
STAGE-2Fade the outer VWAP band — balanced days only M-06first classify the day as balanced or migrating, then fade rejections at the 2-standard-deviation VWAP band ONLY on balanced days; kill condition agreed in advance: if its daily P&L just mirrors the two live VWAP strategies, it dies — the balance/migration state feature survives either way [vs F03/C4]
STAGE-2Opening range measured in volume, not minutes M-04freeze the opening range once a set amount of volume has traded (instead of a set number of minutes), then run the standard break play — the one sanctioned experiment with volume-based bars; going any further than this needs an operator ruling [scope shelf OPT-067]
UNBLOCKED · whitelistM-05 futures-reopen 18:00/15:00 ORB retestCHECK 2 verdict (07-11): overnight coverage SUFFICIENT — but ONLY on the whitelisted sources (databento 1m 2016-2024, nq_1min_from_ticks, ohlcv_5m_24h, HUD ≥2026-06); the default DEV corpora are overnight-blind (0–13% ON coverage — the quantified CCM-FVG scar); any run touching cache/ohlcv_5m/ hash corpora or ohlcv_1m DEV files is invalid by construction. Overnight-session SCOPE is still an operator call (DECISION-NEEDED)
Digest battery arms — feed-to-sweep, run order re-ranked
STAGED · run 1stDM-02 velocity-gate ablationruns FIRST; placebo control = random-cancel
STAGEDDM-04 → merged into expansion-D1IB extension fade, 1.2×/1.75× geometry; param donors: port C1 (Nick's IB gates) + B2 IB-break params
BLOCKED · via B8DM-01 POC timeout inversionscheduled after the 1.11 profile build — which is now blocked on 5m inputs by the B8 gate verdict (1m range-uniform required); timeout param comes from claim S6-B4
STAGEDC010-B ES/NQ leader-laggard pairs sleevethe only zero-beta candidate in the package; 2× cost wall expected; trap C8 (pairs-legs-are-not-trades) blocks its stats until wired
DECISION-NEEDEDDM-03 event-candle midpointconditional on operator adjudication of the event-lane 6-NULLs receipt (same adjudication as claims S6-B2/B9); prerequisite feature 1.21 staged
Param enrichment + pointers
STAGED · rowsCommunity param ranges → staged cardsonto D1/DM-04, D2, DM-01, DM-02, bos_retest; the B14822 bos_retest gate atoms (LH-suppression, proximity veto, body veto, zone expiry) go onto X10–X13 — 4 of the biggest-N families, zero condition atoms today
ACTIVETrigger-lab re-sweep (operator-managed)running in a separate terminal out of results/trigger_lab_autonomous_2026_07_11/; feeds bets 2/4 and the corpus; Stage B assembly waits on it (PLAN §9)
KILLED-CLEANSupplier's 12 mechanism self-rejectionsD5-collision, CPI straddle, 5pt-stop GEX bracket, plain band fade, etc. — endorsed, all correct calls; receipts in the graveyard
Law of this lane: entry/stop/target specs must be one-sitting encodable · honest V5-C2 stop geometry · the cost floor is an eligibility filter (skip the trade), never the banned stop-widening denominator trick · decorrelation to the live book checked at gate.

Bug Traps — the quality control that catches fake edges (dest D · 30 checks · first 5 built 2026-07-11)

New surface: the machinery that makes every measured number trustworthy. Delivered in ltm/harness_traps/: 6 test suites — every check passes, and every bug we deliberately planted to prove the traps work was caught (exact tallies: Archive drawer); wiring deferred to WIRING_TODO.md; zero modifications to ltm/v5/ or the running sweep. Sources: TIER1_TRAPS_REPORT.md, SUGGESTIONS_TRIAGE.md §5.

Tier 1 — built & passing 2026-07-11 (78/78 checks · 24 planted bugs all caught)

Each trap is proven by a positive control: we deliberately planted the bug it hunts and confirmed the alarm rings.

The bug it catchesWhat the trap does about itStatusCaught so far
Volume-profile levels (POC/VAH/VAL) computed from bars that are too coarse — the "point of control" you trade against isn't where the volume actually was B8An acceptance gate: builds the profile from 5-minute bars, compares it against a 1-minute reference on 22 real ES sessions from 2024, and refuses the build unless the POC lands within 8 ticks and the value area within 12 ticks on ≥90% of sessions (tolerances committed BEFORE seeing results). A code hook (vp_binning.assert_vp_build_allowed()) physically blocks disallowed builds.BUILT · gate ran honest FAILCaught: EVERY 5m profile method fails — the standard close-binned method misses the true POC by a median 10 ES points (14% pass), and even the best 5m method (82%) misses the bar. All profile builds blocked until 1-minute inputs land; tick-level confirmation queued. Blocks 1.11 / DM-01 / D4.
The backtest fills you at prices the bar never traded, gives you a kind stop fill, or measures the bracket from the wrong price C1+C2Hard asserts on every simulated fill: fill price must be inside the bar's range, stop fills are never favorable, entries always happen on the NEXT bar's open, brackets are always measured from the actual fill. Signal timestamp pinned to the decision bar's CLOSE (the information-availability time). Directly encodes the scar from the 16 strategies killed by fantasy fills.BUILT · 12/12 passCaught: all 8 planted engine bugs, including the exact touch-bar-extreme fill that killed the 16 quarantined streams.
Wrong contract math — wrong dollars-per-point, clock changes shifting the session, a quarterly roll's +63-point price step read as a real move, trading through an early close D5+D6A contract-math lint plus two frozen "known-answer" calendar windows with hand-verified correct answers: a March 2024 week containing both the DST change and a contract roll, and Thanksgiving 2024 with its 13:15 early close. Any pipeline touching them must reproduce the pinned answers.BUILT · 41/41 passCaught: all 13 planted bugs (NQ priced at $5/pt, wrong multiplier, off-grid price, no-early-close, roll-step-as-gap, fixed-UTC session anchor, …).
A feature that quietly rewrites its own past once the future is known — looks prophetic in a backtest, is useless live B7Recomputes every feature with the future chopped off at several depths and asserts the values are bit-identical — turning "we believe nothing peeks ahead" into an automatic test across all 195+ features. Mandatory before the swing-structure and FVG ports land.BUILT · 8/8 passCaught: all 3 planted repainters (full-series z-score, centered moving average, retro-anchored pivot).
One market move counted as a hundred separate wins — overlapping fires inflating the trade count that all the statistics stand on A1Merges overlapping [entry, exit] windows into single episodes (per family and side) and asserts every headline N is an episode count, not a raw fire count. Must run inside the miner's scorer before any multiple-testing math.BUILT · 11/11 passCaught: raw-fire-count-as-N by the assert. Scale of the danger: one family has 127,939 raw events vs 5,302 real trades. The lab's actual trade streams measured clean (deflation ×1.00).
What the traps found when pointed at our existing code and data
FindingWhat it meansStatus
The monthly 1-minute cache (cache/ohlcv_1m/databento_YYYY_MM_1m.parquet) is time-mislabeled F1Real bug: the timestamps are UTC+1 values wearing a New-York label, so the files contain NO regular-session data at all (true coverage ≈ 03:30–09:58 ET). Exposed by a "full RTH session" on Thanksgiving 2025 and bars after the 13:15 early-close halt. Quarantine/rebuild + consumer audit queued (WIRING_TODO #6). The fixture build is unaffected — it uses es_1min_databento_2016_2024.parquet, cross-verified exact.QUARANTINED
Real v4 streams + wired v5 condition families acquitted F2/F5Three real trade streams (C1, C4, F15) pass the fill invariants with zero violations, and the four wired condition families (weekly_ema21, day_type, ny_open_sweep, vix_regime) are bit-identical when the future is truncated.CLEAN
Entry-price convention: 103 of 114 entries match exactly F311 entries differ 0.25–2.25 pt against an independent bar source — consistent with the two frames coming from different data, not yet proven. Re-run on the lab's own frames after the sweep (WIRING_TODO #1).OPEN
Tiers 2–4 + noted — staged, build in this order
TierThe bug it catchesWhat the trap does about itStatus
2 · corpus prepA corrupted or subtly wrong master dataset — gaps, bad resamples, levels silently wrong on roll days, timestamps in the wrong convention (the exact class that caught F1), settlement confused with the last trade, indicators read before their warm-up is doneSix checks that must pass before the 2016-2026 mining corpus is cut: integrity gate B1 · continuity/resample B2 · roll discipline — levels on unadjusted prices, returns on adjusted B3 · timestamp-convention/DST asserts B4 · settlement-vs-close pin B5 · warm-up hygiene B6STAGED
3 · miner/engineRules that are secretly always-true, results that live on one lucky day, orders straddling the session boundary, costs computed on the wrong fee schedule (the cost law currently uses discount-broker rates, not prop-firm rates — quietly important), replays that differ run to run, a pair counted as two tradesDegenerate-condition lint A2 · plateau + spec-jitter arm A3 · rule round-trip identity A4 · PnL-concentration screen A5 · daily-equity metric basis A6 · session-boundary order hygiene C3 · state-dependent slippage + prop-rail fee table C4 · replay determinism C5 · double-entry reconciliation C6 · ES-NQ stale-state assert C7 · pairs-legs-are-not-trades C8STAGED
4 · bet #3Account-simulator math that mis-measures drawdown or assumes actions a prop firm doesn't allowDD-basis unit test D1 · firm action-space table D2 · copy-fidelity/common-mode scenario D3 · block-bootstrap drawdown D4STAGED
notedScenario knobs and policy lines rather than testsE1–E8 accepted as noted: delta-feed provenance E5 is a hard prerequisite of feature C17 · the weekly shadow-parity replay E6 goes into the SHADOW-READY definition-of-doneNOTED
digestProcess holes: features silently differing between training and live, results that hinge on the exact clock minute, costs leaking into entry counts, prop rules drifting, multi-condition rules with freeloading conditions6 hardenings: feature-manifest assertion at train AND inference · phase-jitter arm (±15m/±1 bar) · slippage-eligibility audit (costs affect PnL only, never entry counts) · prop-rule adversarial-drift scenarios · marginal-contribution + sample floors for multi-condition rules · walk-forward boundary-position policySTAGED
Adopted statistical upgrades (from the 2026-07-11 217-option review — plain-English detail in the Adopted Gaps drawer)
The failure it guards againstWhat it addsStatus
Killing a real edge because the sample was simply too small to see it — shipping a premature negativePower / minimum-detectable-effect analysis on every card; when the sample cannot resolve the claimed edge the verdict is INCONCLUSIVE, never KILL OPT-126ADOPTED-GAP #1
Declaring forecast A "better" than forecast B by eyeballA proper forecast-comparison test suite (Diebold-Mariano, Giacomini-White, Clark-West for nested models, Pesaran-Timmermann) for the claims queue and the bet-#3 volatility forecasts OPT-114ADOPTED-GAP
Beating a strawman — every idea needs named opponentsAn enumerated baseline battery predeclared per card: random-walk/no-change, do-nothing, incumbent policy, cost-free-vs-cost-aware OPT-113PARTIAL residue
A headline Sharpe that is really just the prize for searching hard enoughDeflated Sharpe Ratio + Probability-of-Backtest-Overfitting reported as cheap add-on stats on anything that survives OPT-115PARTIAL residue
Forgetting how many things were tried — the denominator of every "significant" resultONE immutable global trial ledger across all five bets, including seeds and abandoned runs; every LLM prompt/branch/rejection logged into the same ledger OPT-116 + OPT-106PARTIAL residue
Policy A-vs-B comparisons where the difference is really just different random luckCommon-random-numbers matched replay discipline in the bet-#3 simulator OPT-127PARTIAL residue
Fills that look fine on price but are systematically poisoned — you only get filled when you're about to be wrongPost-fill markout/toxicity measured at fixed horizons, turning shadow/paper parity into an adverse-selection detector; extends E6 in the SHADOW-READY definition-of-done OPT-053ADOPTED-GAP
An LLM "predicting" history it already read during trainingA contamination protocol — pinned weights/prompts/tools, point-in-time retrieval rebuild, future-event probes, renamed entities/shifted dates/masked numbers; precondition for the planned LLM stage. Prompt-hiding does NOT fix look-ahead (OPT-176) OPT-138ADOPTED-GAP · precondition
A frozen model quietly going stale — alpha decays in ~12 months and the plan freezes one config with no aging policyA temporal-decay / refit-policy study: train-once signal aging vs rolling/expanding refits, run as a post-survivor robustness read (PLAN §8) OPT-202 = XS-12ADOPTED-GAP
A survivor feature that only works because of a confounderDouble/debiased-ML stability probes as nuisance controls — explicitly NOT causal proof, NOT invariance training (the IRM variant stays dead per REC §8.4) OPT-098ADOPTED-GAP · probe
Passive entries whose fill odds are fantasyA fill-survival model (competing risks: filled / cancelled / adverse move) built from OUR OWN shadow/live fill logs — never the banned book data; only matters if passive entries are ever used; pairs with the port-C7 queue haircut OPT-088 = OPT-181ADOPTED-GAP · COND
A student model inheriting future knowledge from a hindsight teacherAn oracle-distillation leakage audit rule: if any teacher-student setup ever appears, the student must not inherit future-trajectory information from a retrospective teacher; zero cost now, blocks a known leak class OPT-189 = MICRO-11ADOPTED-GAP · rule
Look-ahead sneaking in through text or revised economic data; misreading why an edge paysThree doctrine lines: point-in-time language models are the only valid historical-text route OPT-026 · ALFRED macro vintages, never revised FRED OPT-068 · a mispricing-vs-compensated-risk interpretation field in survivor reporting (changes decay/crowding expectations) OPT-033ADOPTED-GAP · doctrine
Adopted claims of unequal quality treated as equal; artifacts silently swapped; sloppy intake of the next research package; conditional policies built on volatility mirages; edges that flip sign across states; simulators graded on charmSix report-and-process fields: an A/B/C/D/X evidence tier on every adopted claim OPT-107 · content hashes + model cards on label/normalizer/model/cost/fill artifacts OPT-142 · the four reading questions as a standing intake checklist + CARD-E vs REC-E namespace discipline OPT-144 · a volatility-tercile behavior report before ANY conditional policy OPT-133 · a sign-flip-across-states report line with multiplicity correction OPT-047 · reality-gap metrics that must PREDICT shadow reconciliation, not just report it OPT-187PARTIAL residue
original cards (expand) — preserved verbatim
Tier 1 — BUILT 07-11 (protects staged builds + every measured number)
BUILT → gate FAILVolume-profile accuracy gate B8tested on 22 real ES sessions from 2024: every way of building a profile from 5-minute bars missed the true point of control badly (median miss 10 ES points on the standard method; even the best method fell short of the accuracy bar committed BEFORE seeing results). Verdict: all profile builds blocked until 1-minute inputs land; a tick-data confirmation is queued [blocks 1.11/DM-01/D4 · exact tolerances: Archive drawer]
BUILT · passFill-engine honesty asserts C1+C2the backtest can never fill you at a price the bar didn't trade, never gives you a favorable stop fill, always enters on the next bar's open, and always measures brackets from the actual fill price — this directly encodes the scar from the 16 strategies killed by fantasy fills; every engine bug planted to test it was caught
BUILT · passContract-math lint + known-answer test weeks D5 + D6two frozen calendar windows with hand-verified correct answers — a March 2024 week containing both a clock change and a contract roll (a +63-point price step), and Thanksgiving 2024 with its 13:15 early close; every bug planted into them was caught
BUILT · passRepaint detector B7recomputes every feature with the future chopped off and asserts the values don't change — turning "we believe nothing peeks ahead" into an automatic test on all 195+ features; mandatory for the swing-structure and FVG ports; the repainting features planted to test it were all caught
BUILT · passEpisode merge — one move, one trade A1merges overlapping fires into single episodes so the miner can't count one market move as a hundred separate wins — the single biggest way trade counts get inflated; must run before any multiple-testing math [WIRING_TODO #4]. The lab's actual trade streams are already clean; the raw fire sets are the danger (one family: 127,939 raw events vs 5,302 real trades)
Findings from running the traps against existing code/data
QUARANTINED · F1databento-1m monthly cache (cache/ohlcv_1m/databento_YYYY_MM_1m.parquet)real bug: timestamps are UTC+1 values mislabeled as America/New_York — the files contain NO RTH data at all (true coverage ≈ 03:30–09:58 ET); exposed by a "full RTH session" on Thanksgiving 2025 and bars after the 13:15 early-close halt. Quarantine/rebuild + consumer audit queued (WIRING_TODO #6). The fixture build is unaffected — it uses es_1min_databento_2016_2024.parquet, cross-verified exact
CLEAN · F2/F5Real v4 streams + wired v5 condition families acquittedC1/C4/F15 pass the fill invariants with zero violations; weekly_ema21, day_type, ny_open_sweep, vix_regime bit-identical under truncation
OPEN · F3Entry-price convention 103/114 exact11 entries differ 0.25–2.25pt vs an independent bar source — consistent with frame-source differences, not yet proven; re-run on the lab's own frames post-sweep (WIRING_TODO #1)
Tiers 2–4 + noted (staged, build in this order)
STAGED · Tier 2Corpus prep (before the 2016-2026 mining corpus is cut)B1 integrity gate · B2 continuity/resample · B3 roll discipline (levels-on-unadjusted / returns-on-adjusted — levels are silently wrong on roll days without it) · B4 stamp-convention/DST asserts (the class that caught F1) · B5 settlement-vs-close pin · B6 warm-up hygiene
STAGED · Tier 3Miner/engine hardeningA2 degenerate-condition lint · A3 plateau+spec-jitter arm · A4 rule round-trip identity · A5 PnL-concentration screen · A6 daily-equity metric basis · C3 session-boundary order hygiene · C4 state-dependent slippage + prop-rail fee table (the cost law is currently computed on discount-broker RT — quietly important) · C5 replay determinism · C6 double-entry reconciliation · C7 ES-NQ stale-state assert · C8 pairs-legs-are-not-trades
STAGED · Tier 4Bet-#3 trapsD1 DD-basis unit test · D2 firm action-space table · D3 copy-fidelity/common-mode scenario · D4 block-bootstrap DD
NOTED · E1–E8Scenario knobs / policy linesE5 delta-feed provenance = hard prerequisite of feature C17 · E6 shadow-parity weekly replay goes into the SHADOW-READY definition-of-done
