STAGED · digest6 digest process hardeningsfeature-manifest assertion at train AND inference · phase-jitter arm (±15m/±1-bar) · slippage-eligibility audit (costs affect PnL only, never entry counts) · prop-rule adversarial-drift scenarios · marginal-contribution + sample floors for multi-condition rules · WF boundary-position policy
Adopted process gaps 2026-07-11 (from the 217-option review)
ADOPTED-GAP #1OPT-126 power/MDE analysis + explicit INCONCLUSIVE verdictswhen the sample cannot resolve the claimed edge, the verdict is INCONCLUSIVE, not KILL — the statistical enforcement of "never ship a premature negative"; goes on the card template + harness
ADOPTED-GAPOPT-114 forecast-comparison test suiteDiebold-Mariano, Giacomini-White, Clark-West (nested), Pesaran-Timmermann — a proper "is forecast A better than B" test for the claims queue + bet-#3 vol forecasts
PARTIAL residueOPT-113 enumerated baseline batterypredeclared per card, not ad hoc: random-walk/no-change, do-nothing, incumbent policy, cost-free-vs-cost-aware
PARTIAL residueOPT-115 DSR + PBO for headline survivorsDeflated Sharpe Ratio + Probability-of-Backtest-Overfitting reported as cheap add-on stats on anything that survives
PARTIAL residueOPT-116 global trial-denominator ledgerONE immutable search count across all five bets incl. seeds and abandoned runs (currently per-card/per-null); OPT-106 residue folds in: every LLM prompt/branch/rejection logged into the same ledger
PARTIAL residueOPT-127 common-random-numbers matched replayCRN/matched-input discipline for policy A-vs-B comparisons in the bet-#3 simulator
ADOPTED-GAPOPT-053 post-fill markout/toxicity at fixed horizonsturns shadow/paper parity into an adverse-selection detector, not just a fill-price check — extends E6 in the SHADOW-READY definition-of-done
ADOPTED-GAP · preconditionOPT-138 LLM contamination protocolpin weights/prompts/tools/retrieval, PIT retrieval rebuild, future-event probes, renamed entities/shifted dates/masked numbers — precondition for the planned LLM stage on the verbalizable frozen set; prompt-hiding does NOT fix look-ahead (OPT-176)
ADOPTED-GAPOPT-202 temporal-decay / refit-policy studytrain-once sequential-cohort signal aging vs rolling/expanding refits (=XS-12) — alpha decays ~12mo (house prior) and the plan freezes ONE config with no aging policy; runs as a post-survivor robustness read (PLAN §8)
ADOPTED-GAP · probeOPT-098 double/debiased-ML stability probesnuisance-control stability read on survivor features — explicitly NOT causal proof, NOT invariance training (the IRM variant stays dead per REC §8.4)
ADOPTED-GAP · CONDOPT-088 fill-survival model (competing risks)time-to-fill/cancel/adverse-move from OWN shadow/live fill logs — never MBO, so LPC #1 untouched (=OPT-181/MICRO-3); only matters if passive entries are ever used; pairs with the port-C7 queue haircut
ADOPTED-GAP · ruleOPT-189 oracle-distillation leakage auditif any teacher-student setup ever appears, the student must not inherit future-trajectory information from a retrospective teacher (=MICRO-11) — zero cost now, blocks a known leak class
ADOPTED-GAP · doctrineOPT-026 + OPT-068 + OPT-033 doctrine linesPIT language models = the only valid historical-text route · ALFRED macro vintages, never revised FRED · a mispricing-vs-compensated-risk interpretation field in survivor reporting (changes decay/crowding expectations)
PARTIAL residueOPT-107 evidence-tier field · OPT-142 hashes+model cards · OPT-144 intake checklist · OPT-133 vol-tercile precondition · OPT-047 inversion scan · OPT-187 reality-gap scorecardA/B/C/D/X tier on every adopted claim · systematic content-hashing of label/normalizer/model/cost/fill artifacts + model cards · the four reading questions as a standing intake checklist + CARD-E vs REC-E namespace discipline · vol-tercile behavior report before ANY conditional policy · sign-flip-across-states report line with multiplicity correction · conditional-fidelity metrics that must PREDICT shadow reconciliation
Sequencing law: Tier-1 traps + the B8/B7 gates land BEFORE the 1.11 profile build and the bet-#1 miner scoring respectively. Spec pins are committed before results (B8 tolerances, C2 signal_ts = decision-bar close, A1 overlap = trade windows, real-stream checks on dev bucket ≤2025-10-01 only).

Claims To Verify — fact-checking mined claims on our own data (dest F · 37 items, priority-ordered)

New surface: 35 S6 claims + 2 T1 claims queued for batch verification on OUR data (vendor numbers never trusted; each claim is a prior attached to a specific card). Standing custody: definitions pinned before the batch · selection statistics end 2026-03-31 · FDR-within-group. T2-1..11 logged only (T2-5/T2-6 dig-worthy if idle); T3/T4 = documentation-only.

In priority order — every claim below is somebody else's number until it survives on our own 2016-2026 data.

  1. Does a level get stronger or weaker with each touch? The answer decides whether "more touches" ADDS to or SUBTRACTS from the shipped defended-level score, and settles the related ported-tool question — either result changes a live feature. ⚠ Queued for the operator's eyes: the result may contradict his own "more touches = more significance" doctrine. A12 / X8 PRI 1 · ⚠ operator
  2. How far do initial-balance extensions actually run, and does the edge decay with how long the break took? Supplies the entry/target priors and a minutes-to-break gate for the top battery arm (the IB extension fade, D1/DM-04). Vendor stats over 12 years — re-derived here. A1 PRI 2
  3. How often do the stop and the target get hit inside the SAME bar? That ambiguity rate calibrates the fill emulation — which changes every measured number in every bet. The vendor tested EUR/USD 1-minute data; we re-derive on ours (1m-for-fills is inside the plan's carve-out). A10 PRI 3
  4. How rare are trend days, and do ES and NQ print them together? The base rate underwrites the trend-day kill-switch (C052) and is the build-priority test for the whole ES-NQ-state feature class. A3 + A4 PRI 4
  5. Does where price closed versus yesterday's point of control predict the next day's break — and does the 80% value-area rule hold? First payoff tests of the profile build (1.11), currently blocked with it on the B8 verdict; the third leg (B4) derives the POC-timeout parameter that DM-01 needs from data. A5 · A6 · B4 PRI 5 · post-1.11
  6. Four session-behavior stats: how often the day stays inside its expected standard-deviation envelope (sizing envelopes for bet #3) · whether the Asia session really runs one-directional (a pre-open bias feature) · how often the day's high is already in by 15:00 (a survival-curve session clock) · the London-midpoint behavior. The London bracket arm carries a fat stated adverse-excursion tail — feature first, mechanism later. A7 · A8 · A9 · A2 PRI 6
  7. Seven cheaper conditioning stats: does IB size flip the regime read · what happens after a gap fills · is there a trend-day precursor signature · the delta-correlation go/no-go that decides whether the C17 backfill is worth it (B5) · intraday excursion envelopes (B6/B7) · the overnight-escape stat · B11–B13. B11 is vendor self-promo — order-of-magnitude read only. A11 · B1 · B3 · B5 · B6/B7 · B10 · B11–B13 PRI 7
  8. Three event-day stats (FOMC rotation, CPI geometry, December afternoons) — verified as STATS only; adopting any mechanism from them sits against the event-lane six-honest-NULLs receipt and needs the same operator adjudication as DM-03. B2 · B9 · B8 PRI 8 · adjudicate
  9. The C-batch of ten cheap claims — run together, corrected for multiple testing (FDR); no card hinges on any of them, and the supplier itself flags C4/C10 as near-certain noise or debunked-family cousins. C1–C10 PRI 9
  10. Does a balance/discovery day-type gate (IB width + VWAP stretch terciles) condition the IB card? The only independent, loss-disclosing lead in 600 records; built on existing inputs. Point-in-time tercile cuts are mandatory — the supplier itself flagged the look-ahead. T1-1 PRI 10
  11. How often do single-print profile levels get filled at D+5/D+10? A new profile sub-object and a target-selection prior; vendor self-promo, runs only after 1.11 unblocks. T1-2 PRI 11 · post-1.11
original cards (expand) — preserved verbatim
Run order (by decision-relevance to the five bets)
PRI 1 · ⚠ operatorDoes a level get stronger or weaker with each touch? A12the answer decides whether "more touches" ADDS to or SUBTRACTS from the defended-level score, and settles the related port question — either result changes a shipped feature, and it may contradict the operator's own "more touches = more significance" doctrine (queued for his eyes) [X8]
PRI 2A1 IB extension cascade + timing decayentry/target priors + a minutes-to-break gate for the top battery arm (D1/DM-04)
PRI 3A10 same-bar TP/SL ambiguity ratescalibrates fill emulation → changes every measured number in every bet; re-derive on our data (vendor tested EUR/USD 1m); 1m-for-fills is inside the plan's carve-out
PRI 4A3 + A4 trend-day rarity & ES/NQ concordancebase rate for the C052 kill-switch + build-priority test for the whole ES-NQ-state class
PRI 5 · post-1.11A5 vpoc-position → next-day break · A6 80%-rule · B4 DM-01 legfirst payoff tests of the 1.11 build (currently blocked with it on B8/1m); B4 gives DM-01 its timeout param from data
PRI 6A7 SD containment/reversal · A8 Asia one-directionality · A9 HOD-by-15:00 survival · A2 London-midsizing envelopes (bet #3), pre-open bias feature, session-clock family; A2's bracket arm carries a fat stated MAE tail — feature first, mechanism later
PRI 7A11 IB-size regime flip · B1 post-gap-fill fork · B3 trend-day precursor · B5 delta-corr go/no-go · B6/B7 excursion envelopes · B10 ETH-escape · B11–B13card conditioning; B5 = the C17-backfill go/no-go; B11 is vendor self-promo, order-of-magnitude only
PRI 8 · adjudicateB2 FOMC rotational · B9 CPI geometry · B8 December afternoonsverify as STATS only — adoption of any mechanism sits against the event-lane 6-NULLs receipt and needs the same operator adjudication as DM-03
PRI 9C1–C10 batchcheap, FDR-corrected, no card hinges on them; C4/C10 near-certain noise / debunked-family cousins (the supplier says so itself)
PRI 10T1-1 discovery/balance tercile gateIB width + VWAP stretch composition on existing 195 inputs, for the D1/DM-04 card; the only independent, loss-disclosing lead in 600 records; point-in-time tercile cuts MANDATORY
PRI 11 · post-1.11T1-2 single-print fill ratesnew profile sub-object + target-selection prior; batch-verify at D+5/D+10; vendor self-promo — run after 1.11 unblocks
Count check: Pri 1–4 = 5 claims · Pri 5 = 3 · Pri 6 = 4 · Pri 7 = 10 · Pri 8 = 3 · Pri 9 = 10 · T1 = 2 → 37 queued. New adopted gap OPT-019 (curve/basis family) adds one more verify when its features land.

Ported Tools — utilities rebuilt from mined trading code (dest E · 21 staged)

New surface: sub-component decompositions of mined indicator/script sources into features, primitives, sweep arms, and harness logic. Adjudication of record: brief rule 5 supersedes the digest's "parked as HYDRA-live-book" stance — ports stay at the SHADOW-READY ceiling, no HYDRA wiring, PineForge execution route rejected (operator confirm item).

Calendar & session
  • Exchange holiday calendar — an offline-generated table of every CME holiday and early close, consumed by the test harness; today our early-close handling is ad hoc. Feeds the session-boundary trap (C3). C6 STAGE-1
  • Cash-hours vs full-session VWAP spread — one genuinely unlogged feature (the gap between the two VWAPs), plus a rule that VWAP is always built from 1-minute inputs — a prophylactic against a silent averaging bug. A6 STAGED
  • Time-of-day extreme hazard — at what clock time the day's high and low tend to print, as a frequency map. B5 STAGED
Levels & zones
  • The full life of an inverted fair-value gap — tracks an FVG from birth, through inversion (a body-close through it flips it into a level), to death (invalidated with a 50-tick buffer — a lifetime rule worth testing on ALL level types), plus a running ledger of which old highs and lows have already been swept. Full source on disk; one sitting to build. A1 IFVG · extends 1.22/1.8/1.9 STAGE-1
  • Nick's initial-balance gates — failure-filter gates for the IB break → extension → retest sequence; the parameter donor for the top battery arm (D1/DM-04). C1 STAGED
  • Candle Majick decomposition — the first-half "battlefield" levels plus the TRAP/CARRY and ABSORB/ROTATE states; ONE build together with setup M-03. The script's hand-tuned score weights were dropped — the right call, no validation was ever shown for them. A3 · = M-03 STAGED
  • Structure-state veto stack + diamond retest — booleans that suppress entries when the market structure argues against them, plus a retest level drawn at the pivot bar's body edge. Its exit logic is EXCLUDED (closed question); percent thresholds re-scaled by ATR. A4 MSP STAGED
  • Sweep & retest grammar — parameterized sweep states B3 · the sweep-and-fail trigger, whose whole game is a confirmed-swing ledger plus a confirmation-lag rule B4 SFP · retest-quality features B7. STAGED
  • Volume-shelf detection — finds the high- and low-volume nodes (busy shelves vs thin air pockets) in the profile; waits behind the B8 profile gate. B6 STAGED · behind B8
  • Settlement level + expected-move bands — the prior settlement price as a magnet level (needs the settlement-vs-close pin, trap B5) and expected-move bands computed from the prior day's option-implied volatility. C2 · C3 STAGED
  • IB-break parameter rows — A/B parameter sets loaded onto the D1/DM-04 card. B2 STAGED · rows
Fills & execution honesty
  • Queue-aware fill rules — a passive limit order only counts as filled if price traded THROUGH it, with an optional volume-based queue haircut and pessimistic same-bar handling; bar data only, no order-book data. Pairs with the fill-engine traps C1/C2. C7 STAGE-1
  • Costed-strategy template — an honest-cost parity configuration for checking TradingView-sourced claims (its own stop fails our cost law — the supplier says so itself). A5 REFERENCE only
Orderflow
  • Volume-spread + manipulation-state toolkit — flags bars where huge volume produced almost no range (someone absorbing everything thrown at them), a 4-state read on whether the tape looks manipulated, and the panic sweep-reclaim detector (same build as the staged feature C13 — build once). Its fake buy/sell-pressure sub-part stays quarantined until real delta data lands. A2 USR V9 · A2c ≡ C13 STAGED
  • Standardized CVD spec — the delta normalization formulas, waiting on the C17 aggressor-flag backfill. B9 CONDITIONAL
Stats & reference
  • MACD-V + volatility estimators — an ATR-normalized MACD C5, and the textbook volatility estimators plus the autocorrelation-corrected Sharpe (merged with the staged S8 feature — same formulas suggested twice) C8 ≡ S1-S8. STAGED
  • TradingStats study checklist — 12 base-rate study specs to replicate on our own data; the vendor's numbers are never trusted. C9 STAGE-2
  • SMT divergence kernel — a reference implementation for the already-planned ES/NQ divergence features (1.5/1.6); admitted not-new. C4 REFERENCE only
  • Gap-fill breakout params — held as a cross-check only; the gap family is settled (79.4%). B8 LOW
  • NR4/NR7 with breakouts — a dedupe miss by the supplier: the compression flags already exist in the 195 features (is_nr4/is_nr7, range_compression_4d/7d); only the days-since-compression counter and the target mechanics survive, as parameter rows. B1 PARTIAL-REJECT
  • The eight repaint exclusions — kernel regression, retro-anchored and assumed pivots, auto trendlines, projection cones, mutating ICT zones: all verified per row as indicators that redraw their own past, all ENDORSED as exclusions. Do not re-propose. X1–X8 KILLED-CLEAN
original cards (expand) — preserved verbatim
Stage-1 (highest reliability per hour)

STAGE-1The full life of an inverted fair-value gap A1 IFVGtracks an FVG from birth through inversion (a body-close through it flips it into a level) to death (invalidated with a 50-tick buffer — a rule worth testing on ALL level types), plus a running ledger of which old highs/lows have already been swept; full source on disk, one sitting to build [extends 1.22/1.8/1.9]
STAGE-1C7 queue-aware fill rulespassive fills require trade-through; optional volume-based queue haircut; pessimistic same-bar — bar-only, no L2; pairs with traps C1/C2

STAGEDC1 Nick's IB gatesfailure-filter gates for IB break→extension→retest; param donor to D1/DM-04 (top battery arm)
STAGED · =M-03A3 Candle Majick decompositionfirst-half battlefield levels + TRAP/CARRY + ABSORB/ROTATE; ONE build with M-03; hand-tuned score weights dropped (correct call)
STAGEDVolume-spread + manipulation-state toolkit A2 USR V9flags bars where huge volume produced almost no range (someone absorbing), a 4-state read on whether the tape looks manipulated, and the panic sweep-reclaim (same build as the staged feature — build once); its fake buy/sell-pressure sub-part stays quarantined until real delta data lands [A2c ≡ C13]
STAGEDA4 MSP veto stack + diamond reteststructure-state suppression booleans; pivot-bar body-edge retest level; exits EXCLUDED; ATR re-scaling of %-thresholds
STAGEDA6 cash-vs-session VWAP spread + VWAP-from-1m ruleone unlogged spread feature (K block lacks it) + a silent-bug prophylactic
STAGEDB3 sweep grammar · B4 SFP · B5 H/L location frequency · B6 VP nodes · B7 retest grammarparameterized sweep states; sweep-and-fail trigger (confirmed-swing ledger + confirmation-lag rule is its whole game); time-of-day extreme hazard; HVN/LVN detection (behind B8); retest-quality features
STAGEDC2 settlement level · C3 expected-move levels · C5 MACD-V · C8 vol estimators + Lo-Sharpeprior-settlement magnet (needs trap B5's settlement-vs-close pin); EM bands from prior-day IV; ATR-normalized MACD; C8 ≡ S1-S8 (merge)
STAGE-2C9 TradingStats module checklist12 base-rate study specs to replicate on our data — numbers never trusted
STAGED · rowsB2 IB-break paramsA/B param rows for D1/DM-04
Reference / conditional / low / rejected
REFERENCE onlyC4 SMT kernel · A5 costed-strategy templatereference implementation for planned 1.5/1.6 · honest-cost parity config for TV-sourced claims (its own stop fails our cost law — supplier says so)
CONDITIONALB9 standardized CVD specdelta normalization formulas — conditional on the C17 delta backfill
LOWB8 gap-fill breakout paramscross-check only; the gap family is settled (79.4%)
PARTIAL-REJECTB1 NR4/NR7 with breakoutsdedupe miss: is_nr4/is_nr7 + range_compression_4d/7d already in the 195 — only days-since-compression + target mechanics survive, as param rows

Count: 21 staged (3 STAGE-1) + 2 reference-only + 1 conditional + 1 low + 1 partial-reject; X1–X8 exclusions endorsed.

Adopted Gaps (2026-07-11) + the full 217-option classification ledger

On 2026-07-11 every research option surfaced by the ML/AI review package (RESEARCH_OPTION_INVENTORY_2026_07_11.md, OPT-001…217) was classified against the plan (GAP_ANALYSIS_2026_07_11.md): COVERED 56 · GAP 24 · PARTIAL 44 · JUDGMENT-CALL 39 · CORRECTLY-EXCLUDED 54. The 24 gaps were adopted (18 distinct after merging Section-6 duplicates — almost all Small effort, mostly harness/doctrine lines). Custody: this classification proposes board changes only; every adoption still goes through the normal card + operator-checkpoint process.

The 18 distinct adopted gaps, in plain English (grouped by what they do)

Referee upgrades — making the judging harder to fool 7 gaps

Never ship a premature negative, enforced by math OPT-126. Before any test runs we compute whether the sample is even big enough to see the claimed edge; when it isn't, the verdict is INCONCLUSIVE — not KILL. This protects real edges from dying to small samples, the single failure mode the operator has been vindicated on repeatedly.

Proper referee tests for "is forecast A better than B" OPT-114. Named statistical tests (Diebold-Mariano and friends) replace eyeballing when two forecasts or models are compared — used by the claims queue and the bet-#3 volatility forecasts.

Anti-luck statistics on every headline survivor OPT-115. Anything that survives gets a Deflated Sharpe Ratio and a probability-of-backtest-overfitting number attached — cheap stats that ask "is this just the prize for searching hard enough?".

Named opponents, declared in advance OPT-113. Every card pre-declares the dumb baselines it must beat — do-nothing, no-change, the incumbent policy, the cost-free version — so nothing wins by beating a strawman chosen after the fact.

One immutable count of everything we ever tried OPT-116 + OPT-106. A single global trial ledger across all five bets, including random seeds, abandoned runs and every LLM prompt and rejection — because "significant" means nothing without the true denominator of attempts.

Matched-luck replays for policy comparisons OPT-127. When account-policy A is compared to policy B in the simulator, both face the exact same random draws, so the difference is the policy — not the dice.

Stability probes on survivor features OPT-098. A nuisance-control read (double/debiased-ML) that asks whether a surviving feature still matters once confounders are controlled — explicitly a robustness probe, not causal proof.

New features — measurement the model doesn't have yet 4 gaps

Candle-range relationship states OPT-044 CRT. A small fixed vocabulary of how the current bar's range relates to the prior one — adjacent to two staged features but not identical; a cheap categorical family for the Stage-B corpus.

The futures curve itself as context OPT-019 = 059/216. Slope, carry, basis convergence, roll yield and open-interest migration computed from the raw dated ES/NQ contracts with causal rolls — a genuinely absent, own-instrument context family; the roll-discipline trap (B3) builds half the plumbing. Adds one claims-queue verify when it lands.

Intrabar path shape OPT-066. Mathematical summaries of HOW price traveled inside the bar (path signatures), admitted only if they beat cheap handcrafted path stats under identical folds.

How one-sided the tape's order flow runs OPT-052. Trade-sign persistence and run-length features — do buyers keep hitting in streaks? — riding the same aggressor-flag backfill as the C17 delta block at near-zero extra cost.

New experiments — models and training regimes 3 gaps

A second opinion on the champion model OPT-097. TabPFN — a model built specifically for small tabular datasets — benchmarked against CatBoost under identical point-in-time folds; it earns a seat only by beating the champion fairly.

Synthetic data, fenced to training only OPT-093 + 139/208. Augmenting TRAINING folds with generated data plus stress scenarios for the bet-#3 simulator — under the hard rule that synthetic rows never appear in validation or test sets.

How fast does a frozen model go stale? OPT-202 = XS-12. Alpha decays in about a year (house prior), yet the plan freezes one configuration with no aging policy. This study measures train-once signal aging against rolling and expanding refits, as a post-survivor robustness read.

Execution & ops — protecting the money path 4 gaps

Are our fills poisoned? OPT-053. Measure what price does at fixed horizons AFTER each fill. If the market systematically moves against us right after we get filled, the fills are adversely selected even when the price looked fine — this turns shadow/paper parity into an adverse-selection detector.

Net the sleeves before the exchange sees them OPT-008 = 194. When multiple sleeves fire opposing orders at the same moment, net them internally first — fewer contracts traded, less cost, same exposure.

Honest odds for passive orders OPT-088 = 181. A competing-risks model of whether a resting order gets filled, cancelled, or run over — built from OUR OWN shadow and live fill logs, never the banned book data; only matters if passive entries are ever used.

An LLM exam it can't have memorized OPT-138. Before any LLM stage runs on historical data: pinned weights and prompts, point-in-time retrieval, future-event probes, and renamed-entity/shifted-date tests — because a model that already read history isn't predicting it. Telling it to "pretend it's 2008" fixes nothing.

No hindsight teachers OPT-189. If a teacher-student model setup ever appears, the student must be audited for inheriting the teacher's knowledge of the future. Costs nothing now; blocks a known leak class.

Doctrine lines — one-sentence laws added to the causality contract 3 gaps (+6 report-field residues)

Historical text only through point-in-time language models OPT-026 · macro data only as originally released (ALFRED vintages), never as later revised OPT-068 · every survivor's report must say WHY it thinks the edge pays — mispricing or compensated risk — because the answer changes how fast it should decay and how crowded it will get OPT-033. Six further report-field residues (evidence tiers, artifact hashes, intake checklist, volatility-tercile precondition, sign-flip scan, reality-gap scorecard) are detailed in the Bug Traps drawer's adopted-upgrades table.

The 24 adopted gaps (ranked: decision-relevance × cheapness; duplicate IDs merged)

The same adoptions as above, one line each with destination and effort — S = small, M = medium.

#OPTWhat it is (one line)Destination surfaceEffort
1OPT-126Power / minimum-detectable-effect analysis + explicit INCONCLUSIVE verdicts when the sample cannot resolve the claimed edgeCard template + validation harnessS
2OPT-114Forecast-comparison test suite (Diebold-Mariano, Giacomini-White, Clark-West, Pesaran-Timmermann)Harness (claims queue + bet-#3 vol forecasts)S
3OPT-100Empirical-Bayes shrinkage for sparse per-strategy/per-regime edge estimatesBet #3 simulator inputsS
4OPT-097TabPFN / small-data tabular prior benchmarked vs CatBoost under identical PIT foldsPhase 4 open model menuS
5OPT-044CRT candle-range relationship categorical feature familyStage B batch-2 / bet-#4 primitiveS

7OPT-066Path signatures / intrabar shape summaries vs cheap handcrafted path statsStage B batch-2 / bet-#4 primitiveM
8OPT-202 (XS-12)Temporal-decay study — train-once sequential-cohort signal aging vs rolling/expanding refitsPost-survivor robustness read (PLAN §8)S-M
9OPT-053Post-fill markout/toxicity measurement at fixed horizonsSHADOW-READY definition-of-done (extends E6)S
10OPT-008 (=194/XS-4)Joint trade/order netting across sleeves before executionBet #3 simulator / ops standards (with ATOM-28/29/30)S-M
11OPT-088 (=181/MICRO-3)Fill-survival model (competing risks) from OWN shadow/live fill logs — not MBOFill-emulation upgrade (pairs with queue haircut)M

13OPT-138LLM contamination protocol (pinned weights/prompts, future-event probes, renamed-entity tests)Harness — precondition for the planned LLM stageS
14OPT-093 (+139, =208/TS-6)Train-fold-only synthetic augmentation + the synthetic-data protocol (never in validation)Phase 4 guarded card; bet-#3 stress generatorsM
15OPT-026PIT language models = the only valid historical-text routeOne doctrine line in the causality contractS
16OPT-068ALFRED macro vintages (never revised FRED)One doctrine line in the causality contractS

18OPT-189 (MICRO-11)Oracle-distillation leakage audit rule (if any teacher-student setup ever appears)Harness, conditional ruleS

Duplicate IDs counted inside the 24-gap bucket: OPT-059 and OPT-216 (= row 6), OPT-194 (= row 10), OPT-181 (= row 11), OPT-139 and OPT-208 (= row 14).

OPT-209TS-7 architecture knockoutPLN-194 (ablation doctrine; applies if deep models ever tried)
OPT-210XA-1 meta-labeler cardPLN-137
PARTIAL — 44 options where the plan covers a narrower version (residue stated; duplicates in parentheses)

Here the plan covers part of the idea; the last column states exactly what remains uncovered and under what condition the residue would be adopted.

OPTOptionCovered byUncovered residue
OPT-002 (=OPT-192/XS-2)Direct cost-aware portfolio allocator across sleevesPLN-003, PLN-057A LEARNED joint weights+abstention layer on realized net utility; SRT downgrade (OPT-173) caps its billing — adopt only when ≥3 independent sleeves exist
OPT-006 (=OPT-204/TS-2, =OPT-212/XA-3)Soft filtered change-point risk overlayPLN-197, PLN-003Continuous size-cut from FILTERED transition posterior (never smoothed); must beat the local HMM-gating negative receipt (OPT-169) as its null
OPT-007 (=OPT-199/XS-9, =OPT-207/TS-5)Uncertainty-aware abstention/sizingPLN-197 sizing-by-score CANDCalibrated intervals / ensemble-disagreement / conformal abstention with drift-aware coverage; kill if coverage collapses under shift
OPT-009 (=OPT-188, =OPT-200, =OPT-213)Same-underlying / local-vs-pooled transferPLN-155, PLN-021 C18The explicit ablation: local vs pooled vs leave-one-contract-out + unrelated-pool negative control — cheap, one experiment (adopted onto Phase 4)
OPT-011 (=OPT-215/XA-6) + OPT-056, OPT-072Options-implied UNSIGNED regime context / surface block / options dataPLN-035, PLN-043, VIX-TS CANDFull PIT options-chain surface (IV skew change, term slope, dispersion, expiry proximity); needs new data + must prove distinct from the killed signed-0DTE lane → operator data-purchase call (scope shelf)
OPT-024LLM structured event extraction (schema-constrained, PIT)PLN-191, PLN-220The concrete extraction-pipeline design; sits against the event-lane 6-NULL receipt — features-only is the safe framing, mechanisms need operator adjudication
OPT-030ETF/index/futures/cash lead-lag linksPLN-053 (C010-B sleeve)Futures-vs-cash / ETF-underlying basis features (needs a cash-index or ETF feed — same parked-external-feed pattern as S11/S12/S14)
OPT-047Conditional inversion scanPLN-004, PLN-117, PLN-120A standard sign-flip-across-states report line with multiplicity correction on ALL staged effects, not just the two queued claims
OPT-048Learned intraday excursion envelopePLN-117 (S6-B6/B7 queued)The learned walk-forward conditional-quantile version vs ToD/ATR/empirical-quantile baselines
OPT-052Trade-sign persistence / order-splitting featuresPLN-008 (C17 delta block)Trade-sign autocorrelation / run-length features from the same aggressor feed; rides the C17 backfill (adopted onto Phase 2)
OPT-086 (=OPT-211/XA-2)HAR/GARCH/EWMA baselines + forecast combinationsPLN-003/122The HAR-first discipline itself: any ML vol forecaster admitted only after HAR/EWMA/GARCH + simple averages incl. high-vol stress (enforces OPT-174)
OPT-087HMM/BOCPD/neural regime routers (filtered only)PLN-197 regime-thresholds CANDSame residue as OPT-006; hard-regime hindsight gating stays dead (OPT-169)
OPT-089Ensemble/seed-disagreement + conformal with drift-aware coveragePLN-162Same residue as OPT-007
OPT-102Monotonic constraints, distributional targets, AFML sample weightingPLN-195, PLN-087, PLN-162Monotonic-constraint option on CatBoost + AFML uniqueness weights for overlapping episodes in TRAINING (episodes currently handled only in scoring)
OPT-103 (=190, =217/XA-8)Robust objectives (downside/CVaR/DRO)PLN-056Strategy-level robust-utility ablation arm in bet #3 (adopted onto Phase 5)
OPT-105Local-vs-pooled training regimesSame as OPT-009Same residue (the negative-control arm)
OPT-106LLM as extractor / hypothesis-generator only, fully loggedPLN-166, PLN-167Formal logging of every LLM prompt/branch/rejection into the trial ledger (folded into OPT-116 on the harness)
OPT-107Evidence-tier labeling of claimsPLN-166An explicit A/B/C/D/X evidence-tier field on each adopted claim/card (on the harness surface)
OPT-113Mandatory baseline batteryPLN-161, PLN-005The ENUMERATED battery per card, predeclared (on the harness surface)
OPT-115Multiplicity control familyPLN-165, PLN-004DSR + PBO reported for headline survivors (on the harness surface)
OPT-116Global immutable trial ledger (full denominator)PLN-166One GLOBAL search-denominator count across all five bets incl. seeds and abandoned runs (on the harness surface)
OPT-127Common-random-numbers matched replayPLN-003CRN discipline for policy A-vs-B comparisons (on the harness surface)
OPT-133Volatility-tercile behavior reportPLN-120 (T1-1)Make it a standard PRECONDITION for any conditional policy, not just the one queued claim
OPT-141 (=OPT-184/MICRO-6)Queue/latency sensitivity matrixPLN-095Latency-dimension sensitivity — only if passive/latency-sensitive entries are ever used; near-moot for a market-order 5m program
OPT-142Content hashes + model cards everywherePLN-176, PLN-092, PLN-178Systematic hashing of label/normalizer/model/cost-engine/fill-policy artifacts + model-card reporting (on the harness surface)
OPT-144Literature-intake checklist + namespace disciplineLPC #12; the triage process itselfThe four reading questions as a STANDING intake checklist for future adoptions (on the harness surface)
OPT-187 (MICRO-9)Simulator reality-gap scorecardPLN-091 (E6 weekly replay)Preregistered conditional-fidelity metrics that must PREDICT shadow reconciliation, not just report it
OPT-205 (TS-3)Stationary order-flow features experimentPLN-008 (C17)Trade-flow side covered; book-OFI side excluded (LPC #1) — residue is only the formal vs-raw-candle ablation of C17
OPT-214 (XA-5)Structurally lagged cross-asset graphPLN-021 (C18), staged externalsThe directed-dated-graph FORMALISM with rewired-graph controls — low value over the flat lagged-state features already staged
Tally check (must sum to 217): COVERED 56 · GAP 24 (18 distinct) · PARTIAL 44 (31 primary + 13 duplicate IDs) · JUDGMENT-CALL 39 (→ the SCOPE DECISIONS shelf) · CORRECTLY-EXCLUDED 54 (→ the GRAVEYARD). No gap requires new data purchases except the options-surface residue (operator call), and none reopens any LPC row.

⚠ REQUIRES OPERATOR SCOPE DECISION — 39 judgment-call options

These are NOT candidates and must not be built speculatively. Each is alive in the literature but sits on a tension only the operator can resolve — almost all one tension: universe expansion beyond ES/NQ (prime directive + LPC §3 require explicit opt-in), plus a handful of killed-lane-adjacent designs where "genuinely new mechanism class" is arguable. Every reopen also requires an explicit falsifier + clean custody + full trial accounting (LPC §4).

Universe expansion — needs an explicit ES/NQ-scope opt-in (29)

Should this program trade anything other than ES and NQ?

That single question decides all 29 options below in one stroke. Each is alive in the literature — stock ranking, FX, crypto, options, bonds, news text, satellite data — but the prime directive says ES/NQ only unless you explicitly opt in, and most also need data feeds we would have to buy. Until you rule otherwise, every row stays on the shelf: nothing here may be built, prototyped, or "just explored". The table lists each option and its specific version of the tension.

OPTOptionStated tension
OPT-012PIT textual novelty strategies (liquid equities)Universe expansion beyond ES/NQ — prime-directive scope + LPC §3 require explicit operator opt-in
OPT-013GKX-style cross-sectional stock rankingSame scope tension (equities)
OPT-014 (=195/XS-5)Constrained AP/P-TreesEquities application needs scope opt-in; the futures analog (policy trees) is already minting route #1
OPT-018Pooled futures trend/momentum sequence policiesConflicts with "sequence-model proposals LAST" and multi-contract pooling scope; decay documented
OPT-022Option-return / VRP MLOptions-alpha universe expansion + documented cost-collapse at full quoted spread
OPT-023Option characteristics → stock returnsEquities/options scope; unreplicated 2026 result
OPT-025LLM headline scoring for driftText data MISSING + sits against the event-lane 6-NULL receipt; equities-calibrated evidence
OPT-027Alternative-data operating nowcastsVendor data purchases + firm-level (equities) scope
OPT-028 (=196/XS-6)Network/graph propagation with economic edgesEquities graph data; PIT-graph construction risk; scope opt-in
OPT-029Attention / disagreement / search-intensity proxiesExternal social/search feeds + sign instability; scope call
OPT-039FX mechanismsUniverse expansion (FX)
OPT-040Crypto as LOB/agent laboratoryUniverse expansion; crypto→ES/NQ transfer requires own-asset baselines (OPT-153); lab value hypothesis-only
OPT-041Treasury / corporate-bond cross-section MLUniverse expansion (rates/credit); ZN-as-context already covered separately (PLN-038)
OPT-061Equity characteristic familiesEquities scope
OPT-062PIT text-derived firm featuresEquities + text data scope
OPT-063Alternative-data sources (card/web/satellite/…)Vendor-vintage contracts + scope
OPT-064Search/social attention dataFeed purchase + contradictory evidence; scope
OPT-073EDGAR / PhraseBank / FiQA corporaText/equities scope
OPT-074Crypto venue feedsScope (lab data only)
OPT-075Open cross-sectional libraries + Qlib infraEquities infra; harmless but pointless without the scope decision
OPT-076Earnings-call audio / vocal dataText/audio scope + heavy caveats
OPT-079Small NN ranking/utility headsEquity extension of CARD-E14; scope + NN-late doctrine
OPT-082IPCAEquities factor modeling; scope
OPT-083Conditional autoencoder asset pricingEquities; scope
OPT-084Adversarial SDF estimationEquities; scope
OPT-085Deep parametric portfolio policiesEquities + unreplicated 2026; scope
OPT-096GNNs over dated economic graphsSame as OPT-028
OPT-195 (XS-5)Constrained P-Tree falsification cardMirrors OPT-014
OPT-196 (XS-6)Cross-market graph falsification cardMirrors OPT-028
Killed-lane-adjacent — needs a "genuinely new mechanism class" ruling (8)

Each of these sits next to a grave. A lane you killed stays killed unless you personally rule the new proposal a genuinely different animal — so each is phrased as the question you would actually have to answer.

  1. Should "learn the chart's patterns first, per training fold" count as different from the killed Kronos lane? Pre-trained chart embeddings failed 16 of 16 variants and the lane is closed — but these proposals pre-train INSIDE each training fold instead of importing a frozen outside model, which is arguably a different class. Reopening still requires your sign-off plus a pre-committed falsifier. OPT-015 = 090 = 197/XS-7 = 206/TS-4 · LPC #2/§4
  2. Should the multi-level "which level gets touched first" label be ruled a new mechanism class? The original first-touch label was overturned for contamination (PLN-139); the structural multi-level framing is arguably new, but that call is yours, not ours. OPT-045
  3. Should point-in-time embeddings be allowed at all? The price-embedding half runs straight into the Kronos reopen bar; the text half can't exist until you open the text lane in the first place. OPT-065
  4. Should bars ever be cut by information flow instead of the clock? The data constitution says 5m/15m only. The volume-clock opening range (M-04) is the one sanctioned narrow probe — going any further than that one experiment is a constitution change. OPT-067
  5. Should a "maximize Sharpe directly" objective ride bet #5, even though sequence models are last in line? The Deep-Momentum lineage conflicts with the minting order (sequence models LAST, PLN-222); the objective alone might be separable from the lineage. OPT-081
  6. Same Kronos-adjacency question, other flavors: the self-supervised pretraining families (TS2Vec/CoST/…) OPT-090, the latent-state representation card — whose kill-if-it-encodes-the-era clause is good hygiene if ever unparked OPT-197/XS-7, and the train-fold-SSL vs contaminated-control card OPT-206/TS-4. All mirror question 1 and fall with the same ruling.
Out-of-program business (2)
  1. Should we build hedging tools for a derivatives book we don't have? Deep hedging is real research, but AIS holds no derivatives book to hedge — it sits outside the five bets entirely. OPT-020
  2. Should we build option-pricing engines? Neural pricing / PDE / BSDE solvers are computational tooling for a business AIS doesn't run. OPT-021
Open operator items — the questions actually waiting on your call
  1. Should the touch-decay test run, knowing it may contradict your own doctrine? The #1 claim in the verification queue tests whether a level gets stronger or weaker with each touch. Your doctrine says more touches = more significance; the data may disagree — and either answer changes the shipped defended-level score. A12/X8
  2. Should "drain the account and reset" cycling be inside the simulator's action space? It's gray under the prop firm's terms of service. Until you rule, it stays out. S3-03
  3. Should any event-day MECHANISM be adopted, given the lane died with six honest NULLs? The FOMC/CPI statistics get verified either way — that's free. Trading them (including DM-03) needs your ruling that event-structure levels are a genuinely new class. S6-B2/B9 + DM-03
  4. Is an 18:00-reopen session in the program at all? The doctrine is RTH-first. The data-coverage audit for the reopen setup already passed on whitelisted sources — the only thing missing is your scope call. M-05
  5. Will you verify the Topstep terms bundle? Payout caps, hold-time minimums, the purchase throttle, the DDL double-payout option, the Apex bracket-ratio rule — all currently room intel, all entering the benchmark math once confirmed. S3-18 et al.
  6. Should we buy options-chain data? A full point-in-time options surface (IV skew, term slope, dispersion, expiry proximity) needs a new data purchase AND proof it differs from the killed signed-0DTE lane. OPT-011/056/072
  7. Should we open a social-signal lane? Build the fast tweet deck, operator teaches the account-reading, system copies mechanically — needs: data source wiring, point-in-time rules, cost model, falsifier. (proposed by the operator 2026-07-11, so intent is established; scoping is what's missing — non-deleting account shortlist already identified) Social signal deck
  8. Three confirmations: ports stay at the SHADOW-READY ceiling with no live wiring (confirm the rule-5 adjudication) S5 §A · the image-gated weekly stat remains an item you export manually C035/C075 · the 2-year-auction idea stays parked, not killed DM-07.
original cards (expand) — preserved verbatim

DECISION-NEEDEDS3-03 drain-and-reset cyclingTOS-gray — in or out of the simulator action space?

DECISION-NEEDEDM-05 overnight-session scopeis an 18:00-reopen arm in-program at all (RTH-first doctrine)? The data-coverage audit is done (whitelist PASS)

Reading rule for LLMs: nothing on this shelf may be promoted to CANDIDATE, staged, or prototyped without an explicit operator ruling recorded in the plan. "It looks promising in the literature" is not a ruling.

Graveyard — every kill, with kill-confidence

The project's scars, annotated so a stranger (or a stranger's LLM) inherits them ACCURATELY. Every entry: what it was · why it was killed · the receipt · a kill-confidence tag. CLEAN KILL methodologically sound under current standards, receipts cited — treat as permanent absent a genuinely new mechanism class + operator sign-off. OLD-METHOD KILL — re-examinable the verdict predates the 2026 methodology fixes (the 4 trader-encoding false-reject bugs · the retired core-13 single-model tick-bar architecture · the pre-causal-fill-audit era) — the operator explicitly believes some of these were falsely killed; re-examination still requires his sign-off. SUPERSEDED retired for policy/custody/duplication reasons, not falsifying evidence. Tally: 14 killed lanes + 5 closed questions + 13 package-level rejections (= the PLN-216 register of 32) + the 54 correctly-excluded research options (20 live-option collisions + 34 explicit negatives) + 12 legacy-era verdicts + 4 superseded custody items.

1 · Killed-lanes register (PLAN §7 — permanent receipts) — 14
What it wasWhy it diedReceiptKill-confidence
Binary win/loss labelUnlearnable — a coin flip — in the 5-design label shoot-out on the full fire corpus, a fair head-to-head under identical features and splitslabel shoot-out, full fire corpusCLEAN KILL
R-regression labelLearnable but never profitable — same shoot-out, judged on kept-book moneylabel shoot-outCLEAN KILL
MFE-as-target labelMost learnable, worst money — TWICE; it secretly selects volatility. Kept as a forensic tool onlylabel shoot-out ×2CLEAN KILL
Exit-geometry optimizationClosed question on the v4 corpus: the original exits were already near-optimal. Re-proposable only by citing a survivor's early-MAE loss concentrationLPC #9CLEAN KILL
Re-entry captureThe excursion it chases is a volatility mirage; a full re-entry fleet was testedLPC #9CLEAN KILL
Kronos / frozen foundation embeddings16 of 16 variants failed the frozen-embedding gate — the signal was real on its own (0.61) but 93% redundant with what we already had, and it hurt every fold. No foundation-model release reopens this by itselfLPC #2CLEAN KILL
Text-LLM fine-tune routeText pretraining does not transfer to market bars — demoted permanentlyPLN-129CLEAN KILL
Sub-second / MBO / orderbook-depth dataFailed repeatedly at every honest test (including the 2026-05-13 v122 filter NULLs) and is banned by the prime directive: do not acquire, compute, store, or expand. The old "revisit when OOS N≥100" notes are OVERRIDDEN by this ban; reopening needs operator sign-off regardlessLPC #1 · v122 NULLs 2026-05-13CLEAN KILL
Signed 0DTE / gamma-flow timingSigned flow tells you what's happening now, not what happens next — a nowcast, not a forecast; the SPXW and QQQ variants were killed. UNSIGNED risk context remains parked, not killedLPC #10CLEAN KILL
1-minute timeframe as ML/signal domainNo edge over 5 years, the worst cost geometry, and it was 45% of the corpus mass in an untradeable domain. 1m may resolve FILLS only (the A10 carve-out)LPC #7CLEAN KILL
Manual labeling / eye labels as ongoing inputRetired for custody and policy, not label quality: the old-vault selection had leaked (only 5 of 26 picks survived clean reselection) and the prime directive bans the operator's eye as an input. The 680 historical labels remain archive/training materialLPC #4/#8SUPERSEDED
Repainting indicatorsMechanical dishonesty as a class — the value changes after the bar closes. Banned by construction; trap B7 now asserts it automatically in CItrap B7CLEAN KILL
Same-day externals (VIX/GEX/COT same-day values)A look-ahead class. The receipt is the tainted VIX-term-structure incident: #1 importance in all 28 folds while tainted, vanished when properly lagged. Prior-day / real release lag onlyVIX-TS incidentCLEAN KILL
Importance-based feature selection without money-liftThe importance-thief scar: nq_es_spread_zscore improved fold counts and destroyed the holdout (win rate 63→51). Money-lift decides seats, not importance scoresburned-in lesson #5CLEAN KILL
2 · Closed questions / retired roles — 5
What it wasWhy it diedReceiptKill-confidence
Veto judge as the alpha factoryThe judge halves losses but cannot cross zero at useful volume — an honest capped result, not a bug. Retained as a future secondary gate over strategies that are already positive on their ownLPC #3CLEAN KILL (role)
First-touch (+1R-before−1R) labelIts early appeal was label contamination; cleanly anchored it is barely learnable. The structural multi-level variant (OPT-045) sits on the SCOPE shelf — that is an adjudication, not a reopenPLN-139CLEAN KILL (overturned)
"Weak-and-trustworthy auto-ships" doctrineAmended by the Kronos dilution evidence: a feature can be trustworthy AND real and still hurt the book — money-lift decidesPLN-138SUPERSEDED (doctrine)
Event/calendar mechanism laneCleared with 6 honest NULLs — do not re-propose without a new mechanism class. DM-03 and claims S6-B2/B9 sit against this receipt pending operator adjudicationPLN-140CLEAN KILL (lane)
Old Q2 vault (2026-04 → 06-04) as "holy OOS"Selection had peeked at it — demoted to a design-touched robustness check, opened once, read asymmetrically: a fail is an alarm, a pass is only weak evidenceLPC #5/#6SUPERSEDED (custody)
3 · Package-level rejections, 2026-07-10/11 (do-not-re-propose receipts) — 13
What it wasWhy it diedReceiptKill-confidence
Hour-boundary front-run (buy :58, sell :01) DM-05Fails the cost law by construction: a ~3-minute market-in/market-out hold pays 2–3 NQ points round trip against a few points of expected move, with no natural stop — and widening the stop to fix the ratio is the banned trickdigest rejectCLEAN KILL
Fail-fast time-invalidation wrapper DM-06A time-stop is an exit atom, and exit geometry is a closed question. Re-proposable only by citing a survivor's early-MAE loss concentrationexit-geometry laneCLEAN KILL

Exit-layer policy atoms ATOM-01…06/08/09/10/31Closed exit question. ATOM-07 is note-only; ATOM-21/22/23 (adds, pyramiding, probe-then-full) are PARKED as outside the single-entry program, not killedclosed exit questionCLEAN KILL
The 117-claim batch as a batchA multiple-testing swamp — testing 117 claims at once guarantees lucky imposters. Superseded by the sanctioned per-card queue with FDR-within-groupS6 queue designSUPERSEDED

OPT-156No mechanism-free indicator-zoo / symbolic-expression mining without trial ledgerPROG §2; PLN-165/166 are the sanctioned versionCLEAN
OPT-157No raw prices / raw levels / era-identifying clocks as featuresPROG §2; stationarity lesson (PLN-162)CLEAN
OPT-158No unfinished HTF bars, touch-bar extremes, same-bar hindsight fills, pre-publication text, same-day options OIPROG §2; PLN-084/086/135CLEAN
OPT-159Do not reopen exit/re-entry giveback without a genuinely different mechanismPLN-126/127; LPC #9CLEAN
OPT-160No tiny per-family exceptions before global basket/TF policies winPROG §2CLEAN
OPT-161Do not spend 2025 CONFIRM or inspect 2026 to decide what to try — protected periods die on inspectionPROG §2/§6; PLN-157/158CLEAN
OPT-162MBO expansion killed (100 ms reconstruction proposal superseded)LPC #1; REC §5.2CLEAN
OPT-163Kronos / frozen foundation embeddings killed — version records are provenance, not a reopen signalLPC #2; REC §6/§8.10CLEAN
OPT-164Pooled take/skip meta-filter retired as alpha factoryLPC #3; REC §8.11CLEAN
OPT-165Eye labels forbidden as roster or supervised targetLPC #4/#8 (custody)SUPERSEDED
OPT-1661m ES/NQ killed as ML domain (5m/15m only)LPC #7CLEAN
OPT-167Binary win/loss, R-regression, MFE-target, exit-opt, re-entry — closed label-engineering questionsPLN-123…127; LPC #9CLEAN
OPT-168monster24 stays dead — must not be resurrected from backtest summariesREC §8.15; failed the FORWARD/live gate (forward evidence is the only real judge)CLEAN
OPT-169HMM hard-regime gating reduced performance locally; hindsight hard-regime strategies are the fragile pattern — this tile sits DELIBERATELY next to Phase 5's live "regime thresholds" CANDIDATEREC §8.14; dead-ends archive (daily-resolution HMM states on sub-minute tick bars, retired architecture)RE-EXAMINABLE — old-era resolution mismatch; the sanctioned re-examination is the soft FILTERED change-point overlay (OPT-006/087), which must beat this receipt as its null, with operator sign-off
OPT-170Strategy-001 pool-sweep/reclaim ML route failed its validation gateREC §8.12CLEAN
OPT-171TSFM local negatives — CatBoost beat tested TSFMs pre-cost; separate study negative at 20 bps; DLinear failed the economic bar; IRM failed cross-environmentREC §6/§8.1-8.5CLEAN
OPT-172Practitioner anti-patterns banned — zero-cost/touch-fill Pine backtests, N=4, selection-exposed "private 90%", post-hoc filters without trial countingPRAC §3; PLN-150CLEAN
OPT-173Red-team downgrades respected — Portfolio-ML is NOT "proven frontier"; pooled-LOB universality unproven for futuresSRT corrected claimsCLEAN
OPT-174"ML consistently beats HAR/GARCH and survives crises" — REJECTED (literature contradictory; motivates the HAR-first residue)SRT; TS appendixCLEAN
OPT-175DeepLOB/TLOB classification performance is not alpha; spread-aware labels reduce apparent predictabilitySRT; MICRO (moot under LPC #1)CLEAN
OPT-176Prompt-hiding ("pretend it is 2008") does not fix LLM look-aheadSRT; attached to adopted OPT-138CLEAN
OPT-177Withdrawn Kim–Muhn–Nikolaev financial-statement-LLM paper = zero positive evidenceSRT; arXiv 2407.17866 (withdrawn)CLEAN
OPT-178Vote-counting / shared-data reimplementation ≠ independent replicationSRT coverage warning; intake checklist (OPT-144)CLEAN
6 · Legacy-era verdicts (pre-2026 methodology fixes) — 12 · read these with care

Amber rows are the OLD-METHOD kills — verdicts reached with methodology since found broken. The operator explicitly believes some of these were falsely killed; they are still not open lanes — he decides if and how any is re-asked.

What it wasWhy it died — and why the verdict is suspectReceiptKill-confidence
Short side "structurally dead" (12+ attempts)Every attempt lived inside the retired core-13 single-model tick-bar architecture (R1–R13, 2026-02/03). The label-family map (07-04) lists 3 short framings as the unbuilt frontier — re-encoding as standalone mechanisms needs operator sign-off, not a rerun of the old pipelineR1–R13 · label map 07-04OLD-METHOD KILL
Min-R:R filter "blocks the best trades"True of the retired monster model — its strongest signals were low-R:R near support at 62% win rate — a property of THAT model, not a law. The current cost law supersedes the question at design timeretired monster modelOLD-METHOD KILL
Trailing exits < fixed TP 2:1Old-architecture verdict (including the bugged-then-fixed SuperTrend PF-110 incident) — but note: the CLEAN exit-geometry kill on the v4 corpus closes the practical door anyway; re-exam only via a genuinely different mechanismold architecture · v4 exit killOLD-METHOD KILL
Dual-model < single modelOR/AND/ensemble/independent gates all failed on the retired architecture (2026-02-28). But the modern disjoint-books finding — different labels pick near-disjoint books — is evidence FOR multi-judge voting, now a Phase-5 candidate2026-02-28 testsOLD-METHOD KILL
Calendar features "add noise" (13/28 FAIL)An old-era feature ablation at tick bars. Calendar CONTEXT (FOMC/CPI proximity) is in the 195 today — the CLEAN receipt in this area is the event-MECHANISM lane (6 NULLs), which is a different claimtick-bar ablationOLD-METHOD KILL
HMM regime features at tick bars (13/28 FAIL)Daily-resolution regime states applied to sub-minute bars — a resolution mismatch (trader-encoding bug #2). Same re-exam route as OPT-169: a filtered soft overlay, with operator sign-offtrader-encoding bug #2OLD-METHOD KILL
Vol-adaptive barriers / structural stop verdictsLabel-change tests on the retired model (6/28, 16/28). Bet #2's cost-geometry race is the modern, sanctioned re-ask of the stop-width questionretired-model label testsOLD-METHOD KILL
Trader-methodology REJECTs — Pablo · Smashelito · Firefly · Hsaka (as tested pre-fix)All four verdicts carried one or more of the 4 systematic false-reject bugs: post-model-filter framing · resolution mismatch · single-factor p-values on confluence setups · a 17/28 fold gate on rare events. The operator explicitly believes these were falsely killed. Sanctioned route: standalone encoding at bar level with bootstrap-CI (largely done via the HYDRA setup stack); the Pablo sub-second/MBO retry collides with the MBO ban and stays closedfeedback_trader_methodology_testing_bug.mdOLD-METHOD KILL
MBO sub-second filters on v122 (2026-05-13, 3+3 NULLs)Honest NULLs at a tiny out-of-sample N, with their own "revisit when N≥100" clause — mooted by the later hard MBO ban. Kept as evidence the lane was tested honestly before being banned2026-05-13 · LPC #1SUPERSEDED
NQ-v2 feature phases (reverted)Holdout falsification: win rate 56.7→50.6 despite better fold counts — the origin of the importance-thief lesson. Its descendant C18 family is staged WITH the watchlistholdout falsificationCLEAN KILL
CCM-FVG v0 OOS fail + overnight data gapThe time-of-day edge was an artifact of ~1% overnight coverage in the DEV 5m data. CHECK 2 (07-11) quantified the scar and produced the M-05 data whitelist — the bug class is now a named trapmemory 07-04 · CHECK 2 07-11CLEAN KILL
L2 book features at 5m barsNaN-pattern leakage plus distribution shift — 0 live signals in 32 days after backtesting fine — and the data domain itself is now banned32-day live NULL · LPC #1CLEAN KILL
7 · Superseded custody / policy items — 4
What it wasWhy it was retiredReceiptKill-confidence
April-2026+ as "untouched holy OOS"Under corpus v5, 2026-04-01→06-04 is design-touched robustness data — earlier design choices had already peeked at it; the TRUE vault starts 2026-06-05LPC #5SUPERSEDED
Old final-vault boundaryThe current true vault grows forward and is opened once after ~84 trading days; no rescue tuning follows the readLPC #6SUPERSEDED
Human-state research splits (pre-2025 SELECT / 2025 CONFIRM)Retained only inside that historical lane's own audit; never defines the current roster or vaultLPC §2SUPERSEDED
Digest's "park ports as HYDRA-live-book material"Brief rule 5 (written later) reopened port-logic re-implementation as sub-component decomposition; SHADOW-READY ceiling, no wiringbrief rule 5SUPERSEDED
original cards (expand) — preserved verbatim
1 · Killed-lanes register (PLAN §7 — permanent receipts) — 14
KILLED-CLEANBinary win/loss labelCLEAN KILLunlearnable (coin flip) in the 5-design label shoot-out on the full fire corpus — a fair head-to-head under identical features/splits
KILLED-CLEANR-regression labelCLEAN KILLlearnable but never profitable — same shoot-out, judged on kept-book money
KILLED-CLEANMFE-as-target labelCLEAN KILLmost learnable, worst money — TWICE; it secretly selects volatility; kept as a forensic tool only
KILLED-CLEANExit-geometry optimizationCLEAN KILLclosed question on the v4 corpus: original exits near-optimal (LPC #9); re-proposable only citing a survivor's early-MAE loss concentration
KILLED-CLEANRe-entry captureCLEAN KILLthe excursion it chases is a volatility mirage (LPC #9); full re-entry fleet tested
KILLED-CLEANKronos / frozen foundation embeddingsCLEAN KILL16/16 variants failed the frozen-embedding gate — signal real (0.61 alone) but 93% redundant, hurt every fold (LPC #2); no foundation-model release reopens this by itself
KILLED-CLEANText-LLM fine-tune routeCLEAN KILLtext pretraining does not transfer to market bars — demoted permanently (PLN-129)
KILLED-CLEANSub-second / MBO / orderbook-depth dataCLEAN KILLfailed repeatedly at every honest test (incl. the 2026-05-13 v122 filter NULLs) + prime-directive ban; LPC #1: do not acquire, compute, store, or expand — the old "revisit when OOS N≥100" notes are OVERRIDDEN by this ban; reopening needs operator sign-off regardless
KILLED-CLEANSigned 0DTE / gamma-flow timingCLEAN KILLsigned flow is a nowcast, not a forecast; SPXW+QQQ variants KILLED (LPC #10); UNSIGNED risk context remains parked, not killed
KILLED-CLEAN1m timeframe as ML/signal domainCLEAN KILLno edge over 5 years, worst cost geometry, was 45% of corpus mass in an untradeable domain (LPC #7); 1m may resolve FILLS only (the A10 carve-out)
SUPERSEDEDManual labeling / eye labels as ongoing inputSUPERSEDEDcustody + policy, not label quality: old-vault selection leaked (only 5/26 picks survived clean reselection) and the prime directive bans the operator's eye as an input; the 680 historical labels remain archive/training material (LPC #4/#8)
KILLED-CLEANRepainting indicatorsCLEAN KILLmechanical dishonesty class (value changes after the bar) — banned by construction; trap B7 now asserts it in CI
KILLED-CLEANSame-day externals (VIX/GEX/COT same-day values)CLEAN KILLlook-ahead class — the tainted VIX-term-structure incident is the receipt (#1 importance in all 28 folds when tainted, vanished when lagged); prior-day / real release lag only
KILLED-CLEANImportance-based feature selection without money-liftCLEAN KILLthe importance-thief scar: nq_es_spread_zscore improved fold counts, destroyed holdout (WR 63→51); money-lift decides seats
2 · Closed questions / retired roles — 5
KILLED-CLEAN · roleVeto judge as the alpha factoryCLEAN KILLhalves losses but cannot cross zero at useful volume (LPC #3) — an honest capped result, not a bug; retained as a future secondary gate over already-positive strategies
KILLED-CLEAN · overturnedFirst-touch (+1R-before−1R) labelCLEAN KILLearly appeal was label contamination; cleanly anchored it is barely learnable (PLN-139). The structural multi-level variant (OPT-045) sits on the SCOPE shelf — that is an adjudication, not a reopen
SUPERSEDED · doctrine"Weak-and-trustworthy auto-ships"SUPERSEDEDamended by the Kronos dilution evidence: trustworthy AND real can still hurt — money-lift decides (PLN-138)
KILLED-CLEAN · laneEvent/calendar mechanism laneCLEAN KILLcleared with 6 honest NULLs (PLN-140) — do not re-propose without a new mechanism class; DM-03 and claims S6-B2/B9 sit against this receipt pending operator adjudication
SUPERSEDED · custodyOld Q2 vault (2026-04 → 06-04) as "holy OOS"SUPERSEDEDselection had peeked — demoted to a design-touched robustness check, opened once, read asymmetrically (fail = alarm, pass = weak evidence) (LPC #5/#6)
3 · Package-level rejections, 2026-07-10/11 (do-not-re-propose receipts) — 13
KILLED-CLEANDM-05 hour-boundary front-run (:58→:01)CLEAN KILLfails the cost law by construction (digest reject)
KILLED-CLEANDM-06 fail-fast time-invalidation wrapperCLEAN KILLexit-geometry killed lane; ledger note: re-proposable only citing a survivor's early-MAE loss concentration
SUPERSEDED · parkDM-07 2y-auction demand gateSUPERSEDEDout of program shape (new feed, overnight hold, daily TF), not falsified — ledger-park, operator optionality
KILLED-CLEANFeature 1.18 bar3_reversal_exitCLEAN KILLexit layer (closed question)
KILLED-CLEANExit-layer policy atoms ATOM-01…06/08/09/10/31CLEAN KILLclosed exit question; ATOM-07 note-only; ATOM-21/22/23 (adds/pyramiding/probe-then-full) PARKED as outside the single-entry program, not killed
SUPERSEDEDThe 117-claim batch as a batchSUPERSEDEDmultiple-testing swamp — superseded by the sanctioned per-card S6 queue with FDR-within-group
KILLED-CLEANPort-logic X1–X8 repaint exclusionsCLEAN KILLkernel regression, retro-anchored/assumed pivots, auto trendlines, projection cones, mutating ICT zones — verified per row, endorsed
KILLED-CLEANSupplier's 12 mechanism self-rejections + ~80 exit-stack recordsCLEAN KILLD5-collision, CPI straddle, 5pt-stop GEX bracket, plain band fade, etc. — endorsed, all correct calls; named-and-dropped
KILLED-CLEANFantasy-fill space (PF 70 · 100% WR · Renko/HA · no-stop systems)CLEAN KILLquarantined as documentation (S7 T4) — fill fantasies, not strategies
SUPERSEDED · routePineForge execution route for portsSUPERSEDEDpolicy: sub-component decomposition only, no HYDRA wiring
SUPERSEDED · dupNR4/NR7 port (B1) · C16 premarket_rvolSUPERSEDEDboth duplicates of existing features (is_nr4/is_nr7 + range_compression; rvol_cum_tod) — dropped, param-row residue survives
KILLED-CLEANZarattini momentum band / OHLCV momentum bandsCLEAN KILLfails all 4 promotion gates (memory 07-04); bar-level directional entry signals = structural dead category on ES/NQ — presume cost-dead
KILLED-CLEANNon-causal touch-veto stream family (16 staged streams)CLEAN KILLfill-on-touch-bar-extreme bug class (memory 07-06) — all dead; causal-fill audit now mandatory and encoded as traps C1/C2
4 · Correctly-excluded research options — live-option collisions (20 OPT ids in 8 tiles)
KILLED-CLEAN · LPC #1Book-data microstructure chain — OPT-004, OPT-049, OPT-050, OPT-051, OPT-070CLEAN KILLstationary OFI → fill survival → toxicity → execution, queue-imbalance/microprice, depth/cancel features, LOB data acquisition — all require banned depth/MBO data
KILLED-CLEAN · LPC #1Market-making + learned residual — OPT-016 (=OPT-185/MICRO-7)CLEAN KILLrequires book/queue data and an out-of-program business
KILLED-CLEAN · LPC #1DeepLOB lineage benchmarks — OPT-080CLEAN KILLbanned data domain + classification ≠ alpha (OPT-175)
KILLED-CLEAN · LPC #1LOB simulators + validity checklist + triangulation — OPT-094, OPT-136, OPT-140 (=OPT-186/MICRO-8)CLEAN KILLcalibration requires the banned depth lane; bar-level replay needs are met by trap C5 + judge-in-loop replay
KILLED-CLEAN · LPC #1OFI benchmark + event-horizon latency map — OPT-179, OPT-180 (MICRO-1/2)CLEAN KILLbook data + sub-second horizons
KILLED-CLEANBehavioral event-drift mechanisms — OPT-032CLEAN KILLthe event/calendar lane cleared with 6 honest NULLs (PLN-140)
KILLED-CLEANTSFM alpha priors — OPT-036, OPT-037, OPT-038, OPT-091, OPT-092CLEAN KILLgeneric + finance TSFM zoos and Chronos-2 panel transfer: local CatBoost-beat-TSFM negatives (OPT-171) + LPC #2/#3 — no foundation-model release reopens lanes by itself
SUPERSEDED · LPC #11"Package QA upgrades a record to alpha evidence"SUPERSEDEDQA is structural only — parsing/hashes/counts never validate design quality or profitability
6 · Legacy-era verdicts (pre-2026 methodology fixes) — 12 · read these with care
KILLED-REEXAMINABLEShort side "structurally dead" (12+ attempts)OLD-METHOD KILLevery attempt lived inside the retired core-13 single-model tick-bar architecture (R1–R13, 2026-02/03); the label-family map (07-04) lists 3 short framings as the unbuilt frontier — re-encoding as standalone mechanisms needs operator sign-off, not a rerun of the old pipeline
KILLED-REEXAMINABLEMin-R:R filter "blocks the best trades"OLD-METHOD KILLtrue of the retired monster model (its strongest signals were low-R:R near support, 62% WR) — a property of THAT model, not a law; the current cost law supersedes the question at design time
KILLED-REEXAMINABLETrailing exits < fixed TP 2:1OLD-METHOD KILLold-architecture verdict (incl. the bugged-then-fixed SuperTrend PF-110 incident) — but note: the CLEAN exit-geometry kill on the v4 corpus closes the practical door; re-exam only via a genuinely different mechanism
KILLED-REEXAMINABLEDual-model < single modelOLD-METHOD KILLOR/AND/ensemble/independent gates all failed on the retired architecture (2026-02-28); the disjoint-books finding (different labels pick near-disjoint books) is modern evidence FOR multi-judge voting — Phase 5 CAND
KILLED-REEXAMINABLECalendar features "add noise" (13/28 FAIL)OLD-METHOD KILLold-era feature ablation at tick bars; calendar CONTEXT (FOMC/CPI proximity) is in the 195 today — the CLEAN receipt in this area is the event-MECHANISM lane (6 NULLs), a different claim
KILLED-REEXAMINABLEHMM regime features at tick bars (13/28 FAIL)OLD-METHOD KILLdaily-resolution states on sub-minute bars = resolution mismatch (trader-encoding bug #2); same re-exam route as OPT-169: filtered soft overlay, operator sign-off
KILLED-REEXAMINABLEVol-adaptive barriers / structural SL verdictsOLD-METHOD KILLlabel-change tests on the retired model (6/28, 16/28); bet #2's cost-geometry race is the modern, sanctioned re-ask of the stop-width question
KILLED-REEXAMINABLETrader-methodology REJECTs — Pablo · Smashelito · Firefly · Hsaka (as tested pre-fix)OLD-METHOD KILLall four verdicts carried one or more of the 4 systematic false-reject bugs (post-model-filter framing · resolution mismatch · single-factor p-values on confluence setups · 17/28 fold gate on rare events) — memory receipt feedback_trader_methodology_testing_bug.md; the operator explicitly believes these were falsely killed. Sanctioned route: standalone encoding at bar level with bootstrap-CI (largely done via the HYDRA setup stack); the Pablo sub-second/MBO-resolution retry collides with the MBO ban and stays closed
SUPERSEDEDMBO sub-second filters on v122 (2026-05-13, 3+3 NULLs)SUPERSEDEDhonest NULLs at tiny OOS N with their own "revisit when N≥100" clause — mooted by the later hard MBO ban (LPC #1); kept as evidence the lane was tested honestly before being banned
KILLED-CLEANNQ-v2 feature phases (reverted)CLEAN KILLholdout falsification: WR 56.7→50.6 despite better fold counts — the origin of the importance-thief lesson; its descendant C18 family is staged WITH the watchlist
KILLED-CLEANCCM-FVG v0 OOS fail + overnight data gapCLEAN KILLthe ToD edge was an artifact of ~1% overnight coverage in DEV 5m (07-04); CHECK 2 (07-11) quantified the scar and produced the M-05 whitelist — the bug class is now a named trap
KILLED-CLEANL2 book features at 5m barsCLEAN KILLNaN-pattern leakage + distribution shift (0 live signals in 32 days after backtesting fine) — and the data domain itself is now banned (LPC #1)
7 · Superseded custody / policy items — 4
SUPERSEDEDApril-2026+ as "untouched holy OOS"SUPERSEDEDunder corpus v5, 2026-04-01→06-04 is design-touched robustness data (LPC #5); the TRUE vault starts 2026-06-05
SUPERSEDEDOld final-vault boundarySUPERSEDEDthe current true vault grows forward and is opened once after ~84 trading days; no rescue tuning follows (LPC #6)
SUPERSEDEDHuman-state research splits (pre-2025 SELECT / 2025 CONFIRM)SUPERSEDEDretained only inside that historical lane's own audit; never defines the current roster or vault (LPC §2)
SUPERSEDEDDigest's "park ports as HYDRA-live-book material"SUPERSEDEDbrief rule 5 (written later) reopened port-logic re-implementation as sub-component decomposition; SHADOW-READY ceiling, no wiring
Inheritance rule (repeat, because it is load-bearing): CLEAN KILL entries are permanent receipts — re-proposal requires a genuinely new mechanism class, an explicit falsifier, clean custody, full trial accounting, AND operator approval. RE-EXAMINABLE entries are not open lanes — they are flagged scars whose verdicts used retired methodology; the operator decides if and how any is re-asked. SUPERSEDED entries were never falsified — do not cite them as evidence a mechanism failed.

Appendix — Sources & Data

The 46-strategy baseline roster (honest engine, real costs, 5 yrs). Periods: Train = 2021→2025 (per-family start in the Since column) · OOS = 2026 Q1 · True OOS = sealed (counts only — outcomes never touched). PF/WR reported SEPARATELY for Train and OOS; OOS columns are small samples — wide error bars. These are UNFILTERED base rates — training material, not deploy recommendations; sub-1.0 PF = contrast material by design.

Where the evidence comes from
Corpus build151k entry signals from 46 strategy families, replayed 2021-2026 on a bug-audited engine with real transaction costs
Judge evaluationwalk-forward quarterly folds, shuffle-label controls, pre-committed thresholds
Closed experimentslabel shoot-out (5 designs) · error autopsy · foundation-model gate · exit-geometry sweep · re-entry fleet · feature-toxicity purge
Independent reviewsecond-opinion audit by a separate frontier model (2 rounds): cost-geometry finding, vault-custody finding, strategy-mining roadmap
Mistake ledgerrunning register of every diagnosed failure mode and whether its fix was verified

Appendix — Research Library & Reading Room

Every major research document is on the shelves below with its full text embedded — click a shelf, then a file, and read or download it right here, fully offline. The map underneath shows every candidate on the whole board and its current state at a glance.

The literature review
Domain appendices
