Execution reports

  







  
Glossary — plain words

  
Every term of art used on this board, one plain sentence each. No definition here changes any rule — the board text wins if they ever disagree.

  

    
Reading the results

      

        
TermPlain meaning

        
          
PF (profit factor)Total money won divided by total money lost — above 1.0 the thing makes money, below 1.0 it loses.

          
WR (win rate)The percentage of trades that finish green.

          
R / R-multipleProfit or loss measured in units of the initial risk — a +2R trade made twice what it risked; −1R is a full stop-out.

          
ExpectancyThe average result per trade, wins and losses combined — what one "typical" trade is worth.

          
Drawdown (DD)How far the account has fallen from its high-water mark.

          
MAE / MFEThe worst point a trade went against you (MAE) and the best point it went for you (MFE) while it was open.

          
SharpeReturn compared to how bumpy the ride was — higher means smoother money.

          
CalmarReturn compared to the worst drawdown — higher means the gains dwarf the worst dip.

          
Quintile / tercile / quantileJust buckets: split trades into fifths (quintiles) or thirds (terciles) after sorting them by something.

          
NThe sample size — how many trades or events a number is based on; small N = wide error bars.

        
      

    

    
Testing honestly

      

        
TermPlain meaning

        
          
BacktestReplaying a rule on historical data to see how it would have traded.

          
In-sample / OOSIn-sample is the data the system was built on; out-of-sample (OOS) is data it never saw — only OOS results count for anything.

          
HoldoutData deliberately locked away during development so it can serve as one honest exam later; once you look, it's spent.

          
Vault / custodyThe strictest holdout: data nobody has touched in ANY design decision, plus the paper trail proving that; "custody breach" means someone peeked.

          
DEVThe development data — the sandbox where ideas get built and abused before facing anything sealed.

          
Walk-forwardTrain on the past, test on the next slice of time, roll forward, repeat — mimics how you'd actually have lived it.

          
FoldOne of those train/test slices; "passes 10/16 folds" means it worked in 10 of the 16 time slices.

          
Leak / look-aheadAny way tomorrow's information sneaks into today's decision — the cardinal sin; it makes backtests look brilliant and live trading fail.

          
RepaintAn indicator that quietly redraws its past values once the future is known — looks prophetic on a chart, is useless live.

          
Permutation testScramble the data many times and see how often dumb luck matches your result — if luck does it easily, you have nothing.

          
p-valueThe chance that pure luck produces a result at least this good — smaller is better; 0.05 means luck does it 1 time in 20.

          
FDRFalse-discovery-rate control: when you test hundreds of ideas, some pass by luck — this math keeps the lucky imposters out.

          
Multiple testingThe problem FDR solves: test enough ideas and something ALWAYS looks great by chance.

          
BootstrapReshuffle-and-resample the actual trade history thousands of times to see the range of outcomes you could just as easily have gotten.

          
CI (confidence interval)The honest range around a number — "PF 1.3, CI 0.9–1.7" means the true value could plausibly be anywhere in there.

          
Shuffle controlRetrain the model on deliberately scrambled labels — if it still "works", the pipeline is cheating somewhere.

          
Positive controlPlant a known bug or fake edge on purpose and confirm the test CATCHES it — proof the alarm system actually rings.

          
FalsifierA kill condition written down BEFORE the test: "if X happens, this idea is dead" — no moving the goalposts afterwards.

          
OverfittingA rule that memorized the past instead of learning something real — great backtest, dead on arrival live.

          
StationarityWhether a measurement means the same thing across eras — raw prices don't (ES at 2000 vs 6000), ratios and z-scores do.

          
z-scoreHow unusual a value is versus its own recent normal, measured in standard deviations.

          
KS test / PSIStatistical checks that a data feed or feature still behaves the way it did historically — drift detectors.

          
RegimeThe market's prevailing personality — trending vs chopping, quiet vs violent; edges often live or die by it.

          
Null (honest NULL)A test that found nothing — recorded as a real answer, not retried until it flatters.

        
      

    

    
Market terms

      

        
TermPlain meaning

        
          
Volume profileA sideways histogram of how much volume traded at each PRICE (not each time).

          
POC / VAH / VALFrom the profile: the price with the most volume (point of control), and the top and bottom of the zone holding ~70% of the volume (value area high/low).

          
HVN / LVNHigh- and low-volume nodes: price shelves where lots of business was done (magnets) vs thin air pockets (price slips through).

          
TPO / dwell timeAcceptance measured in TIME at a price instead of volume at a price.

          
FVG (fair value gap)A three-bar gap where price moved so fast it left an untraded window — often revisited later.

          
Sweep / reclaimPrice pokes through an obvious high or low (running the stops there), fails, and comes back — the failure is the signal.

          
IB (initial balance)The high-low range of the first hour of the regular session.

          
ORB / opening rangeSame idea over the first N minutes; the ORB play trades the break of that range.

          
VWAPThe volume-weighted average price of the session — the day's "fair price" line big players benchmark against.

          
EMAA moving average that weights recent bars more — the operator's framework runs on the 21 EMA across timeframes.

          
BOS (break of structure)Price taking out the last meaningful swing high or low — the trend's skeleton cracking.

          
Swing / pivot (confirmed)A local high or low that only counts once enough later bars prove it — before that it can still be erased.

          
SMT / divergenceES and NQ disagreeing — one makes a new extreme, the other refuses; often flags a fake move.

          
Delta / aggressorBuy volume minus sell volume, where "buyer" means whoever crossed the spread to get filled NOW.

          
AbsorptionHeavy hitting into a level that refuses to move — someone big is taking everything thrown at them.

          
CVDCumulative volume delta — the running total of buy-minus-sell pressure through the day.

          
RVOLRelative volume: today's volume versus what's normal for this exact time of day.

          
ATRAverage true range — the going rate for how far bars travel; the standard yardstick for stops and targets.

          
Tick dataEvery individual trade as it happened, not bundled into bars.

          
MBO / L2 / order bookThe queue of resting orders at every price and every change to it — BANNED in this project; only executed trades may be used.

          
GEX / dealer gammaA measure of how options dealers' forced hedging will push the market — high positive tends to pin price, negative tends to amplify moves.

          
0DTEOptions expiring the same day — their hedging flows shove intraday price around.

          
OPEXOptions expiration dates — the calendar around them has validated statistical effects.

          
COTThe weekly government report on what big traders are positioned in — always used with its real publication delay.

          
Roll / rolloverFutures contracts expire quarterly; positions "roll" to the next contract, and the price steps between contracts — mishandled rolls silently corrupt levels.

          
SettlementThe exchange's official daily closing price — not always the same as the last trade.

          
RTH / ONRegular trading hours (the 9:30–16:00 pit session) vs the overnight session.

        
      

    

    
Execution, accounts & project words

      

        
TermPlain meaning

        
          
FillThe actual price your order executed at.

          
SlippageThe difference between the price you wanted and the fill you got.

          
Cost lawThis project's design rule: commissions + slippage must eat no more than 10% of the stop distance — built into the trade, not filtered afterwards.

          
Standing ordersStop and target resting at the exchange from entry — the only exit style that can't cheat in a backtest (signal-based exits react a bar late).

          
Trailing floor / $2k trailThe prop-firm drawdown line that follows your equity up: fall back to it and the account is dead.

          
Day-stopA hard rule that stops all trading for the day once a set amount is lost.

          
Prop firm / eval / fundedA firm (e.g. Topstep) that lets you trade its money once you pass an evaluation; "funded" means you passed and now split profits.

          
The benchmarkThis project's yardstick for everything: the probability that at least one account gets funded within 10 trading days.

          
Shadow / paper tradingRunning a strategy live with no money on it, to see if reality matches the backtest.

          
SHADOW-READYThe ceiling for everything on this board: fully specified and fit to shadow-trade — deliberately NOT wired into the live stack.

          
DecorrelationProving a new strategy doesn't just win and lose on the same days as ones already running — otherwise it adds risk, not diversification.

          
FeatureOne measured input the model can see — "distance to yesterday's high", "relative volume", etc.; this project has ~195.

          
LabelThe definition of "good" the model is taught to predict — changing the label changes what skill it learns.

          
Judge / filterThe model's first job: score an existing strategy's signal as worth taking or not.

          
MintingThe model's next job: WRITE new mechanical strategies itself instead of filtering existing ones.

          
CatBoostThe specific decision-tree ML engine that is the current champion model.

          
BasketA family grouping of strategies (mean-reversion / breakout / hybrid).

          
CorpusThe full set of historical trade events the model trains on.

          
Episode clusteringMerging overlapping signals into one event so a single move can't be counted as many wins.

          
Meta-labelingA second model that doesn't pick trades — it only judges whether to take the first system's trade, and how big.

          
Triple-barrierA labeling method: every trade ends at target, stop, or a time limit — whichever comes first.

          
PIT (point-in-time)Using only what was actually knowable at that moment — e.g. the economic number as first released, not as later revised (ALFRED "vintages" preserve those originals).

          
BackfillRebuilding a data field across history from raw records — always verified before anything is built on it.

          
GateA pass/fail checkpoint a strategy must clear before moving up a tier — see the 4 promotion gates and Road to Live.

          
TrapThis project's word for an automated test that catches a specific known bug class before it can poison a result.

        
      

    

  







  
Archive — Corpus & QA Stats

  
Receipts proving where the research came from. For auditors and LLMs; nothing here is needed to use this board.

  

    
Research-corpus acquisition counts (relocated from the Research Library appendix)

      

        
ItemFigures

        
          
Bounded 2026 snapshot (catalog\)64/64 API requests · 40,586 raw query rows · 32,557 unique source records · 31,857 canonical discovered candidates · 27,844 DOI / 7,117 arXiv / 30,375 Semantic Scholar IDs · deterministic offline rebuild verified · QA PASS, zero errors

          
Expanded core (catalog_v2, current)335,918 canonical candidates · 375,150 source records (CATALOG_V2_QA.json / RUN_MANIFEST.json). Staged supplements NOT merged into the core: ACL 752 · PMLR 25,507. NBER remains blocked (no completed supplement); OpenAlex v1 rejected, refined v2 pending; MLSys = separate unresolved obligation

          
Not embedded in this page (size)the 31,857-row discovery catalog itself

          
NON-CLAIMS (quote this whenever quoting the counts)These are DISCOVERY counts from an auditable, bounded acquisition system — not validated-relevance counts, not read papers, not reproducibility, and not tradable alpha. No single file contains the raw corpus. Do not claim worldwide exhaustiveness or paper-level relevance validation from corpus size alone.

        
      

    

    
QA & spot-check tallies (relocated from drawer text)

      

        
ItemFigures

        
          
07-11 adoption package provenance (SUGGESTIONS_TRIAGE.md)18/18 provenance spot-checks PASS · 4 supplier self-check failures caught

          
Tier-1 validation traps (TIER1_TRAPS_REPORT.md)6 test suites, 78/78 checks passing · 24 planted-bug positive controls all firing · per suite: B8 6/6 (→ gate FAIL verdict) · C1+C2 12/12 (8 planted engine bugs caught, incl. touch-bar-extreme fill) · D5 24/24 + D6 17/17 (13 planted bugs all caught) · B7 8/8 (3 planted repainters caught) · A1 11/11

          
B8 volume-profile gate — pinned tolerances & measured errorstolerances committed BEFORE results: POC 8 ticks / VA 12 ticks / 90% pass bar · measured: close-binned POC median error 40 ticks (10 ES pts) FAIL · range-uniform 82% < 90% FAIL · body-weighted FAIL

          
Feature purge lab (Phase 2)12/12 suspect features acquitted, zero toxics

          
A1 episode-merge deflation referencelab TRADE streams deflation ×1.00 (already episode-clean); raw miner fire sets are the inflation channel

        
      

    

    
What the project took from the package / rejected (relocated verbatim from the Research Library drawer)

      
What this project took from the package: risk-forecast-aware (vol-first) sizing into the account-policy bet · five extra validation controls · the 2026-07-11 adoption package (24 gap adoptions + PARTIAL residues onto the harness) · independent confirmation of the cost-geometry law, the strategy-first direction, and every killed lane. Its orderbook-data recommendations were rejected — banned data (LPC #1). Three separate grading scales appear in these texts and must never be mixed: literature tiers A/B/C/D/X (X = withdrawn, contributes zero evidence) · mechanism Evidence scores 1–5 (orthogonal to AIS fit — e.g. OPT-012 text novelty: Evid 4, AIS fit 2) · red-team confidence Tiers 1–4 (several headline claims were downgraded — respect the downgrades, OPT-173/OPT-107). Handoff rule: read the recovery + policy documents BEFORE treating any historical proposal as current AIS work; killed-lane reversals need a new mechanism class + falsifier + clean custody + trial accounting + operator approval. Corpus acquisition counts and QA receipts live in the Archive — Corpus & QA Stats drawer; the discovery catalog itself is too large to embed — its counts and caveats are there too.

    

    
Where the deeper raw detail lives

      

        
Full QA textsrequest-level tallies, content hashes, byte/line counts, and parse-reconciliation detail are preserved verbatim inside the embedded full texts in the Research Library shelves (PACKAGE_QA.md, README.md, COVERAGE_GAP_AUDIT.md) — nothing was deleted, only moved out of the main reading path.

      

    

  





THE RULES (every option above obeys):
sealed final exam opened once · fold-majority testing · shuffle/sabotage controls · thresholds committed before results · 4 feature trust tests · causality contract (no look-ahead, real costs, standing orders) · session-clustered statistics · mistake ledger + autopsy loop · suspicion of success · full-pipeline null tests for mining · power/MDE with INCONCLUSIVE verdicts (07-11) · predeclared baseline battery · global trial-denominator ledger. Catches so far: fabricated calendar dates, 6 clock-cheaters, 4 engine bugs, a mid-run PF-8 fabrication, repainting indicators, the vault-custody breach itself — and from the 07-11 trap build: the databento-1m monthly cache time-mislabel (UTC+1 stamped as NY — no RTH data at all, QUARANTINED), close-binned volume profile off by 10 ES points (all 5m VP methods now gated), and the raw-fire N-inflation channel in the all-bar miner (episode merge now mandatory before FDR).



Status 2026-07-11: filter era complete (loss-reducer, capped) · strategy-first era beginning (all-bar minting, cost-aware geometry, policy simulator) · corpus v5 rebuilding with clean custody · 07-11 adoption package staged across destinations A–F (~140 items) · Tier-1 validation traps built, all passing (wiring pending) · 24 research gaps adopted from the 217-option review · 39 options on the operator scope shelf. All numbers out-of-sample, never the sealed vault. Not investment advice.
